A01.title: Lorent, the Kingdom of the Red Rose
A01.lore:
"On my honour, I swear eternal love and allegiance to the Rose, the King, and to the Four Virtues of Lady Ioriel."
-Final verse of the Lorentish Knight's Vow

Primer
Lorent has sat as the rightful sovereign of Western Cannor (known as Lencenor) since long before the arrival of the elves in 1000 AA.
It is a country famed for its etiquette, bountiful vineyards and romantic tales of chivalry, but also as one of the Great Powers of Cannor, competing with Gawed to the north and the Empire of Anbennar to the east for dominion over the continent of Cannor.

The kingdom owes its inception and name to Lorenan the Great, an ancient human king who famously defeated the invading Alenic ancestors of its now-rival Gawed during the chaos of the Dragonwake in 470. In much recent history, however, Lorent has become known as a great adopter of elven culture and customs, falling under the guidance of the elf Ioriel in 1022, the wife of then King Ruben II, leading to Lorent becoming the cultural and military superpower it is today.

The country is divided into two primary cultures: High Lorentish (the half-elven nobility and urban human class who have fully embraced the elven ways) and the Low Lorentish (the rural backbone of the country who are still predominantly human, though more accepting of the elvenized customs than its other human counterparts elsewhere). In terms of other races, Lorent has long acted as suzerain for the Redfoot Halflings of the southern Small Country, and maintains close relations with its ancient Ruby Dwarf neighbours of Rubyhold. The Elfrealm of the Redglades also lies under Lorentish control, a woodland sanctuary established by Ioriel herself.

Recent History
The Lilac Wars (1348-1443) between Lorent and Anbennar has brought the kingdom to its knees. And while Lorent did emerge victorious, destroying the House of Silmuna and preventing them from holding the imperial throne ever again, Kylian VI now finds himself at a fragile peace at home. The war saw Lorent's most powerful vassals, the Wine Lords of Madelaire, Eilísin, and Rubenaire, side with the Silmuna, and despite the war's end, they have now refused to swear fealty to their rightful overlord, eroding the authority of the Lorentish crown.

To the east, Lorent has installed its ally, Lothane, the Grand Duke of Wex, on the imperial throne of Anbennar. In the name of unity, Kylian has wed his sister to this new emperor, hoping to use dynastic ties to control the Empire of Anbennar from within, but only time will tell if this is a match meant to last, now that the House of Silmuna, the enemy of their enemy, is no more.

To the north, the halfling Small Country remains calm yet divided between Lorent and its eternal rival, Gawed. Peace never lasts, however, and as Lorent looks to recover from the Lilac Wars, the northern eagle of Gawed begins to look south once again like the days of Lorenan the Great.

A04.title: The Marquisate of Wesdam
A04.lore:
"No one knows what to make of the young Marquis. He seems competent, but the mind of a child can become many things."
-Cevis Orthosmere, half-elven tutor of Laurens Silmuna, November 11th, 1444

Primer
The Duchy of Wesdam was created from de jure Carnetori Kingdom lands by the Kingdom of Carneter-Dameria. This was since the Damerian Kingdom was theoretically an elected role, whereas the Carnetori Kingdom was not, and was ruled by the Lorenti House of Rubentis at the time. After the decline of the Rubentis in 898 and the rise of its cadet house, the House of Dameris, to the throne of Carneter-Dameria, the duchy was eventually given to their loyal follower William the Westward, who would found the House of Wesdam.

Wesdam did little of note before the founding of the Empire of Anbennar, when it was designated a Marquisate and a military frontier against the kings of Lorent. The family síl Wesdam, the holders of the title of Dukes, then became the Marquis of Wesdam. With this newfound prestige, the síl Wesdam family married into the great Silver House of Silmuna, becoming a cadet branch of the family. For hundreds of years, the family of Silmuna-Wesdam ruled the Marquisate, but slowly began to chafe under the overlordship of their relatives, who treated them as simply vassals rather than family.

Recent History
"Vincen and Adénn have made it clear I mean nothing to them. So I am happy to replace them." -Marion síl Silmuna-Wesdam

When the Lilac Wars began in 1348, following the election of Reán Siloriel, King of Lorent to the Imperial throne, Wesdam at first threw their lot in with their Silmuna brethren. However, with the third war beginning in 1407, there was a shift in attitude within Wesdam. Throughout the war, Adénn Skylance treated the Wesdamerian branch of his family and their holdings as a pawn in his battles against the Lorentish, and the war had a grueling cost to the people of Wesdam, which acted as the site of many notable battles.

This came to a head when Adénn demanded that Marion give him control of the Wesdam military and other concessions, citing that he was Marion's feudal lord. This brashness pushed Marion over the edge, and he reached out to the Lorentish to make a deal. Promising to grant the Marquis the throne of Dameria in exchange for his loyalty, in 1409, at a battle known as the Treachery of Wesdam, the Wesdam army and a cadre of Lorentish knights routed the Imperial army, with only a quarter escaping. Following this, Marion helped plan the Battle of the Moon Council, orchestrating the election of Lothane síl Wex as the emperor. However, he died of plague soon after, which many believed was a punishment for treachery against his kin.

His heir, Cristof, would prove an even more fascinating figure, eloping with the Duchess of Madelaire, Reánna, defying noble conventions regarding marriage and courting. He would die before the war finished, leaving his son Erlan as ruler. Erlan would then die during the siege of the Imperial Capital Anbenncóst, in the war's dying days, after slaying Adénn Skylance.

He would leave behind his six-year-old brother Laurens as Marquis under their mother's regency. Wesdam now sits in limbo, without direction. Laurens can follow one of two paths: his Lorentish blood from his Wine Lord mother or his Damerian heritage. Wesdam has made many decisions over the past centuries that have reshaped their fate and that of Cannor, this will undoubtedly be the same.

A05.title: Reveria, the Reavers stuck in the middle.
A05.lore:
"The Lorentish don't fear breaching the Portroy Agreement, but they do fear being breached by our axes!"
-Famous part of King Lothane III's inauguration speech

Primer
Reveria is a kingdom formed during the Era of Black Ice (768 - 978 A.A.) by Reavers from Gerudia who raided and ultimately settled these lands. The kingdom itself, along with its ancient dynasty and capital, owes its name to this ancestry.

Reveria is famous for its fearsome warriors and distinct culture, which is a unique mix between Gerudian and Lencori traditions and customs. The state has legally secured its southern border by forcing the Lorentish to sign the Portroy Agreement in their moment of weakness during the Era of Black Ice, ensuring the land this side of the Portroy river belongs to Reveria. Reveria has both halfling and gnome minorities in its current domain but used to rule over a vaste swathe of originally-gnomish land provinces back when the Gnomish Pass was still under their control.

Recent History
The northern border is a volatile one. For hundreds of years the Reverians have had tense relations with the kobolds through their previous border in the Gnomish Pass. Although Reveria has ruled over the Gnomish Pass for centuries, it has been wrested from their control by the Cliff Gnomes of Nimscodd in 1396, when they had the support of the Gawedis. Reconquering the Gnomish Pass has been the first item on any agenda ever since.

If Reveria is to survive the ages, it will have to either carefully maintain the balance between itself and the two giants of Lorent and Gawed or uproot that balance entirely, coming out on top. Who Reveria ends up allying themselves to is perhaps the greatest decision one can make: will they seek allies in the south, where they have supported the Lorentish in the Lilac Wars not a year prior, using a thankful Lorent to cement an alliance? Or will Reveria align itself with Gawed, who they have fought against as recently as 1396 but is the only viable option to defend oneself against Lorent?

Reveria is approaching a tumultuous time with no heir to the throne, an old king, a war against the gnomes on the horizon and nothing but their axes to hold Gawed and Lorent at bay.

A06.lore:
Primer
The gnomish people long called the mountains and hills of the Dragonheights their home. Isolated and safe, for many centuries the gnomes had little dealing with what they saw as barbaric humans and halflings in the muggy plains to the east and south. Over time, many gnomes ventured south towards the coast, forming new urban centers, which became thriving city-states.

By the 12th century BA these city-states had united to form the Gnomish Hierarchy, one of the ancient empires of antiquity, alongside Castanor and the Kingdom of Kheterata; between these three great powers Western Cannor was divided up, with the Gnomish Hierarchy subjugating the human and halfling tribes of the Small Country, and laying the foundations for Beepeck.

With mercantile prowess and technological cunning, the Hierarchy assisted in the colonization of the Dameshead, helping to build both Vertesk and Damenath. It supported the Damerian Republic in the War of the Cerulean League, though that alliance would be short-lived and the Hierarchy would find itself at war with both the Republic and Castanor for dominance over Cannor at various points.

But everything changed when the kobolds attacked, pouring forth in the years of the Dragonwake. In a few short years, the Hierarchy had been reduced to a single island city-state, Nimscodd, with fully half the gnomish population scattered as exiles through Cannor, and many enslaved.

Recent History
For nearly a thousand years the gnomes have maneuvered to regain their lost lands, and are now on the cusp of a grand effort to do so. Having taken the fortress of Royvibobb with the help of neighboring Gawed, there is a clear front from which to launch the reclamation. But the kobolds in the north are not the only obstacle to be surmounted.

Lorent and Gawed, the great powers of Cannor, stand in constant watch of each other, both eying land across the border with greed. Courting one or the other would secure the borders, but tempt the other's wrath. And beware the mercurial temperament and lifespan of the humans; Gawed's new, young, monarch already wishes to reclaim the Gnomish Pass for his own ends.

To the southwest, the settled reavers of Reveria lurk, and deny the hierarchy land access to the city-state of Portnamm, home of the Creek Gnomes. Directly south of the border sits the Small Country, once colonized by the Hierarchy and now vassals of Gawed and Lorent.

The Hierarchy stands at the foot of its ancient grandness. With adroit maneuvering and the help of the Artificers' Gommo, new inventions and clever tactics will win the day on the Dragon Coast. Settlers are prepared to recolonize their homeland, and volunteers for the Reclamation Army swell. The kobolds are divided amongst themselves, for now, and the gears are already in motion for the first strike.

The time of reclamation has come!

A13.title: Gawed, the Northern Eagle
A13.lore:
"To deny a man your table and hearth is as good as killing him. To steal his little bit of land will end his family. We've never forgotten how to be humane to one another — how to be human. Maybe they have."
-Welyam III Gerwick, on the news of the end of the Lilac Wars.

Primer
For a thousand years, there has always been a Gawed - the proud talon in the eye of the powers to its south and east, the threat they could never ignore nor destroy. Formed by the men of the rough, windswept plains, steaming moors, and close, threatening forests of the north, Gawed has waxed and waned over the years but never been truly conquered.

It has its origins in the Dragonwake, when the city of Vertesk was toppled by Alenic plainsmen. As the Dragonwake worsened, they moved south through the Small Country into the devastated countryside of Lencenor, settling and taking it for their own until driven back north by the unifier-king Lorenan the Great, founder of Lorent, igniting a rivalry that burns to this day.

The Castans' rotting empire could not subdue them; despite the Sorcerer-King Nichmer forcing their southern lands under his puppet-state in Vertesk, the moors and hills remained a front of resistance, and when Castanor crumbled, Gawed rose once more. The White Pestilence avoided its cold realms and allowed it to wrest most of the Small Country from a weakened Lorent, setting flame to the smoldering rivalry, before the influence of Ioriel Redrose choked the House of Gawe and sparked the Decade of Masks, and the civil war that came with it. Since then, Gawed has been ever suspicious of the elves and their vast influence in the southlands.

Recent History
While most of Cannor has burned in the Lilac Wars, the Kingdom of Gawed was stabilizing following the removal of the last Baldfather king, Toman the Mad, and has prospered under the new House of Gerwick. Gawed has long been content to expand its influence through quieter means as it rebuilt from misrule, only briefly moving to crush the Viswall Rebellion alongside its longtime rival in Lorent.

The Greentide and the end of the Lilac Wars, however, have roused the lords of the north to attention. Were it not for Corin's victory in Escann, Gawed might well have been caught sleeping, and the fear of complacency puts dread in every northman's soul.

With the powers to their east destroyed and those in Anbennar to the south exhausted from the Lilac Wars, they may never have such an opportunity again. Gawed's attention again turns outward; to the city-states along the coast of the Giant's Grave to the north, to the long-ruined lands of the gnomes and kobolds to their west, and above all, to Anbennar and Lorent in the south. The old empires of men and elves have driven themselves to exhaustion, and thoroughly wasted their day in the sun. It is time for the Eagle of the North to rise!

A30.title: Wex, Usurpers of the Empire
A30.lore:
"A new era dawns on Anbennar, free of Silmuna tyranny!" -Emperor Lothane III sil Wex

Primer
For as long as they’ve existed, Wexonards have had to fight for every scrap of land and respect they’ve earned. Originating from the north, the Wexonards were driven from their ancestral homeland by the Gawedi during the Dragonwake. What followed was a long and bloody pilgrimage carved across the Damerian Republic, a bygone precursor to the modern Empire of Anbennar.

Multiple times, the Wexonards led the Three Tribes southward, seeking shelter from the Dragonwake and the Gawedi. Every time, they would be denied. Every time, they would have to take what they needed by force until at last, the Wexonards claimed the ancient Castanorian citadel of Bal Hyl. Here, in the crumbling remnants of the Damerian Republic, they settled. And they’ve spent the ensuing centuries slowly expanding, evolving from a petty kingdom of refugees to the Grand Duchy of Wex, one of the strongest powers in the Empire of Anbennar.

Recent History
Since they began in 1348, Wex has been dragged repeatedly into the Lilac Wars, a series of disputes between the House of Silmuna, long-reigning emperors of Anbennar, and the kingdom of Lorent vying for each other’s thrones. For 80 years, proud Wexonards bled and died for the Silmunas’ personal agenda.

Enough. After three such wars, Lothane III sil Wex made his own bid for the Dove Throne of Anbennar. When the emperor passed, Lothane contested his son, Adénn "Skylance" Silmuna’s, claim to the throne, and together with the Silmunas’ Lorentish rivals, he ambushed Adénn and his followers at the Battle of the Moon Council. This act tore the Empire of Anbennar in half, and it would take another two decades of warfare to piece it back together again. But when the dust settled, the Silmunas’ ambitions and excesses had finally been ended and Lothane had earned the Dove Throne.

Wex now stands atop the world, but as ever, the Wexonards must continue to fight to earn their place. Many are the foes that would see them dragged from their newfound throne. Wex is threatened on all sides by the hostile Moon Party, all of whom remain loyal to the fallen House of Silmuna and only bend their knees begrudgingly. Whether they’ll show true fealty to their new emperor or cling to the memory of a dead line is a question Wex will have to answer for them.

The same question of loyalty is echoed within Wex’s own borders. The freshly claimed lands of Dameria are a rich bounty, but its people will not accept Wex’s dominion quietly. And to the north, the Gawedi, ancient conquerors of the Wexonards’ previous home, are poised to sweep into a weakened empire, bringing the longtime rivals into direct conflict once again. Even Wex’s closest ally, Lorent, prizes them mainly as a muzzle for the empire. Lothane owes his current position to the good graces of Lorent’s young king, Kylian VI Siloriel, but would any emperor be content to play the rulers of Anbennar against each other to benefit a foreign king?

A33.title: Verne, Land of Adventure
A33.lore:
"Rejoice, my friend! You live another minute, and none of us can ask for more! Now, come on! Pick that sword back up and follow me, this village is not saved yet!"
-Cristof the Ardent, adventurer-paladin of Verne

Primer
Hail, bold men (and women) of Verne! The whole of Cannor knows you, and cannot help but admire you, for in your heart beats adventure and in your mind the call to do right burns like a torch! The evil quail, and the downtrodden rejoice, when they see the Red Wyvern fly over the field, for there in fine pantaloons and even finer mustaches march the men of Verne, ever striving for something better!

The Duchy of Verne was born the Kingdom of Verne, founded by the great Armoc Vernid, first of the wyvern-riders. Astride these two-legged winged beasts, the Vernmen would forge a prosperous land in the south of what would be one day known as Anbennar, nestled between the Khenak Mountains, the Damesneck Strait, and the great Divenhal Sea. They would rise in fame and renown through their first five centuries, known across Cannor as bold and brave sorts eager for heroics, dramatics, and above all else, adventure. However, the Kingdom would decline as it was "eclipsed by the Moon", the arrival of the Elves and the Wars of Rule forging the Empire of Anbennar which reduced Verne to a Duchy. Worse yet, the beloved wyverns of Verne declined and, generations ago, vanished entirely.

Recent History
Ever loyal, the Vernmen fought for the Moon Party in the Lilac Wars, quelling the Lion Armada of Busilar and holding the southern flank for their brothers-in-arms. Alas, it was not enough to save Dameria, and the victorious Rose left her shattered and the empire in the hands of Wex. Yet Verne's greatest glory has been in fighting the Greentide, vanquished at last. With these wars lost and won, a new chapter in Verne's history begins — Duke Alvar IV looks to new challenges and horizons for his crowded, ambitious people.

To the north, the scattered Vernman city-states call for the restoration of what once was, and Istralore seems to now see a once-friend as a new rival. Across the straits to the west sits a much older rivalry, Pearlsedge, the bald-headed, Reaver-spawn, mariners. To the east Busilar looks avariciously at friendly, island-bound Eborthíl. But between them sit the Hill Gnolls, in the mountains that once were home to wyverns, and across the Divenhal lays Verne's most immediate threat — the slaver-corsairs of Viakkoc, who ravage peaceful settlements and drag off their people for a life in chains!

And if adventure beckons from beyond, from the unknown? Well, such a thing is exactly what Vernmen live for!

A new era begins for the land of the Wyvern. Though there may be darkness in the world, it matters little — against the darkness, the light shines ever brighter! To see what lies beyond, to face what is wrong in the world and set it right — these are the duty of the Bold Men of Verne!

A45.title: Istralore, Marshal of Anbennar
A45.lore:
"Anyone can be a leader. The first follower bears the strongest burden." -An unknown Istralorian commander

Primer
The concept of Istralore began with the arrival of the Remnant Fleet in the year 1000 of the Cannorian calendar. After Munas Moonsinger's bold claims to follow the Moon to a new home for the elves, the first to follow was the ship Estádha, and its leader, Istralania Warsinger. Whether this decision was made on a chance, or through sheer belief, the fleet soon arrived in the war-torn land of Cannor amid the War of the Sorcerer-King. After the landing, a deal was struck: the elves would find a home in Cannor in exchange for their service against Castanor. And in this, Istralania would be one of their greatest weapons. Leading her personal all-female warrior unit, the Istralari Banshees, she would wreak havoc on her enemies.

"Whatever the world may hold for us, my love, it cannot be worse than what we have overcome." -The wedding vows of Sideric 'the Patient' to Istralania.
As the greatest warriors of the free realms prepared to defeat the Sorcerer-King before the Battle of the Trialmount, Istralania began to think of her legacy. So after Urion Starsworn gave his life for her, she knew she had to think about the future. So, after the battle, she began to return the affections of a human knight named Sideric, and the two were soon wed. As thanks for all she had done, Istralania was granted what would be called the Duchy of Istralore by the son of Munas, King Marion Firstborn. The couple's progeny would go on to become the Silver House of Silistra. The Duchy was subject to the Kingdom of Dameria, the heirs of Munas Moonsinger, and would loyally serve them for years.

"In a world of Kings, it is easy to forget the soldiers." -King Marion IV Silmuna
In the year 1221, in the aftermath of the chaos of the collapse of the Jexisian empire, the great mages of the land called a Grand Summit in Aranthíl and formed the Empire of Anbennar. Marion IV, the King of Dameria, was a key supporter of the Mages' efforts to bring peace to the realm. The Duke of Istralore Aucan I supported him in this, volunteering to take up the mantle of his ancestor Istralania and lead the Empire's armies, earning the title of Marshal of the Empire. During the Wars of Rule, Istralore stood by their Damerian lords and helped elevate the nobility to rule over the Empire rather than the mages. As a reward for their contributions, Istralore was given one of the Electorates to represent the nobles of Eastern Dameria.

Recent History
Throughout the entirety of the Lilac Wars, Istralore stayed loyal to the Grand Duchy of Dameria. Against Lorentish warriors and traitors alike, they fought in service to the Silmuna name. But alas, Adénn Silmuna, rightful heir to the Empire, was betrayed by Lothane sil Wex, who sits on the Dove Throne still. And while his ascendancy marked the end of the Grand Duchy of Dameria, Istralore stands as its most legitimate heir, ready to reclaim the legacy of its ancestors.

A49.title: Xanzerbexis, Queen of the Hills
A49.lore:
Primer
The gnolls of Xanzerbexis are the last gnollish holdout north of the Divenhal, and their precarious position is a recent one. At the time of the Landing in 1000 AA, gnollish control spanned nearly all of Businor. However, this dominance was not to last, as local humans and their freshly-arrived elven allies overwhelmed the fractious packs that made up the realm. Eventually the packs were pushed west into the Khenak mountains, where they settled in a disparate patchwork of packs and warlords.

The Khenak proved an inhospitable homeland. Cold, isolated and with next to no arable land, the gnolls of the Khenak warred amongst themselves, slowly consolidating into the 86 packs or what their neighbours called the Hill Gnolls. Hunting, foraging and raiding became the tools of survival, and with typical gnollish resourcefulness, the Hill Gnolls carved out a small niche for themselves in the frontier between a resurgent Busilar and a hostile Verne, loved by neither but useful to both. The 86 packs acted as a useful buffer, and were a rich source of mercenaries for just about any manner of warfare, ranging from local border conflicts to gnollish involvement in the Lilac Wars that would later engulf Cannor.

Recent History
This might have been the fate of the packs of the Khenak: to live a meager existence on the periphery, forgotten by history, and doomed to die in foreign wars. However, the rise of Xanzerbexis, the Queen-of-the-Hill (Golud-en-Vres) may just offer the packs a different fate.

Xanzerbexis herself is an enigmatic figure, stepping out of obscurity to unite the 86 packs into a single state in 1441 AA through a mix of cunning diplomacy and small-scale warfare. Though not a sorcerer, Xanzerbexis enjoys both great personal charisma as well as a razor-sharp intellect. Unusually, Xanzerbexis seems uninterested in donning the demonic mantle of Xhazobine, and with the Khenak united behind her, some whisper that her ambitions are far more mundane. To strike at upstart Busilar, and restore gnollish rule over Businor under a single throne:

The Hillthrone.

Z08.title: Bjarnrík, the Bear of the North
Z08.lore:
Primer
The wind whips around the mountains, tearing the snow from their sides. It weaves through the pine forests, shaking the boughs of ice and rime. It shrieks across the flatlands and over the gray, stormy sea. The men of Dalr fear the wind for its chill, its ferocity, and its Gerudian storms. The men of Cannor fear the North Wind for a very different reason - it brings the men of Dalr.

The kingdoms of the north have long been fractious and broken, but their mastery of the sea and hard-bitten tenacity have made them a threat beyond their numbers and size for centuries. In the Era of Black Ice, they terrorized the Cannorian coast, and even took over the great Empire of Castanor. Greatest among these is Bjarnrík, the Bear of the North, which has always styled itself as the greatest and most forward-thinking of the kingdoms of Dalr. They have reason to think so. From the very beginning, Bjarnrík has learned much from its southern neighbors, when the warrior-queen Bera founded the country on the model of the southern feudal nations -- and with wealth taken from those southerners. Backed by the Skalds, the great religious leaders of the north, Bjarnrík rose in power for three centuries, before the people of Serpentshead landed a stunning reversal on them at Fuglborg, triggering the War of the Bears in 1200 as the jarls fought each other for control of the north.

Recent History
What emerged victorious was Bjarnrík, of course, but her situation is delicate if promising. The Jarls of Sidaett and Revrhavn flaunt their liberties in defiance of the crown, with Revrhavn in particular continuing to reave the southlands to the Konungr's increasing ire. To her north, the Frost Trolls threaten as they have since before there was a Bjarnrík, enslaving captives in their mountain mines. To the east, the fractured Olavish states squabble and skirmish, even as a dangerous new power rises to their east, where the Gray Orcs have crushed the eastern Reach and seem poised to make something entirely new. Across the Frozen Pass to the south sit the city-states of the Giant's Grave: Bayvic, Adshaw, Celmaldor, and the hated Serpentgard. Beyond them lies the vast northern eagle of Gawed, which they cannot but see as a rival and a threat. But beyond all this - beyond the ruins of the Dragon's Coast - lies the remains of the Era of Black Ice. Reveria, Deranne, Pearlsedge - coastal states far from Dalr, but all of which bear Dalric blood and have their roots in her legacy.

Bjarnrík has much to learn from her lost cousins in the south, but in the end she is not the South - she is not defined by what other people do. She is Bjarnrík, the Bear, and the saga of this age is hers to write and sing. The people of Cannor once knew to fear when the seas froze over and the creak of longships could be heard off their coasts. They have forgotten. They will learn again, and soon.

Z15.title: Urviksten, the Black Dragon
Z15.lore:
Primer
"We lie defeated, our empire broken from within and without. On Morban Flats Karadaig was pierced by an arrow. At Bal Mire, my father and Kaston was slain by the warlock. Garrek lies dead by the blades of the Vrorenmarch upstart, and Ebonmarck is lost. But remember this, my son: we earned our place through strength. We have returned home in shame. But are never defeated. Promise me you’ll tend the forge and let it temper your spirit. Gather the Olavish clans, the Skalds from their high-temple, and even the She-Bear's kin. We will return and remind the 'Free Realms' the fear of Black Ice." - deathbed quote of Urvik Ebonfrost to his successor, Alvor Ebonfrost. 

The Wind blows through the mountains, as the forges burn day and night, creating the weapons that will reforge a shattered empire. With fleets of black-as-night sails bringing more and more gold to the war chests and a once revered name is whispered in the halls of Gerudia, Ebonfrost. 

During the legendary Era of Black Ice, when the reavers rode the waves and became the terror of the seas, legendary figures emerged. Names like Derhilda Reaver-Queen, Henrik Divenscourge or Bera Bjarnsson were enough to inspire terror in the hearts of the men of the green lands, but none reached the level of fame and fear of the legendary reaver Kaston Ebonfrost. 

Famous for killing the previous Castan and taking the empire for himself, after his rise to power he changed the trials and turn them into a dynastic affair, where no one not bearing the name of Ebonfrost could take the trials. Several so-called "Wise Men" point out that this was the reason for the fall of Kastonor, foolish of them all, the Ebonfrost Dynasty was the best thing that ever happened to Escann, the civil wars had stopped and Kastonor prospered under the watchful eye of the Black Dragon. 

Urviksten, the birthplace of the greatest dynasty Cannor has ever seen, was occupied until the fall of Kastonor, when Urvik, one of the sons of Kaston the Enthralled regained his home and settled the land along with veterans of the sorcerer king's war, and so the House of Ebonfrost survived the fall of Kastonor yet the reputation of the Ebonfrost was stained and vilified by the sorcerer king, a fact that will never be forgotten.

Recent History
 After recovering Urviksten, the Ebonfrost faced a long period of recovery and waiting to gather the Gerudian refugees fleeing north. They waited for a moment of weakness from their neighbors to reclaim their riches and establish a power base from which to take back what rightfully belonged to them. 

That opportunity presented itself in the year 1410, when the acclaimed Jarl of Nauvsgol died and was succeeded by a weak and corrupt heir. As the new Jarl was crowned, Urviksten began the offensive and although the army of Navusgol was experienced and well equipped, it was crushed by the imposing discipline of the Pale Banners. In the last battle of the Black Pine war, Jarl Karl II Ebonfrost killed the Jarl of Nauvsgol in single combat and exhibited his head on the walls of Urviksten. 

But just as Urviksten ascended, the Graytide began. An army of beastly, primitive creatures poured forth from the Northern Pass and overwhelmed the armies of Vrorenmarch. As quickly as it began, the Ascent of Urviksten was suddenly halted by the new threat. 

Now with the defeat of the Dookanson, the armies of Frozenmaw turn their gaze to the north, looking for any sign of weakness, and thus Urviksten's ambitions have been cut short and currently the Jarldom is in a dangerous situation. Will a dynasty that marked an era of conquest and power fade into obscurity or will the Ebonfrost return to their rightful place and retake their birthright with Ebonsteel and Fire?

Z26.title: Adshaw, the Revenge of the Reach
Z26.lore:
"Their laughs shall turn to screams when they find what this old Alenic has in store for them."
- Lord Rycan Adshaw, the Viper of Rycastle

Primer
Once a mighty kingdom that spanned over all of the Western Alenic Reach, and rivalled the strength of Gawed, the lordship of Adshaw has since fallen far. Around 400 years ago, during the War of the Sorcerer-King the traitorous cowards of Bayvic, declared their independence while Adshaw forces were off on a war campaign, attempting to incite the other Blue Reachmen subject lords into their rebellion. Forced to abandon the war, and losing grace towards Venac and Black Castanor, our forces returned home only to find that it was a little too late. In an effort to avoid the other Blue Reachman lord joining, making the rebellion unwinnable, we were let go of the Bavicman subjugation. Twenty years the peace lasted before the other Blue Reachmen lords rose up. They would have been put into their place had it not been for deceitful elves of Celmaldor. Using cowardly guerilla tactics, their combined forces ambushed ours, and an arrow eventually found its way to our king, marking the end of the kingdom. A special hatred has since been reserved for the Bayvicmen for starting our downfall, and to the Celmaldor for sealing it. Their betrayal has never been forgiven or forgotten.

Recent History
Lord Rycan 'Viper of Rycastle' Adshaw is a patient and cunning ruler. During his reign he has carefully planted spies in positions of influence across the western reach and beyond. The long-eared Maldorians and the money-grubbing coast lords are long overdue for punishment for their wrongdoings. There may have been a time where the proud family of Adshaw were beyond such lies and deceit, but times have changed. And revenge is a dish best served cold.

F22.title: Dartaxes’ Rebellion
F22.lore:
Recent History
King Dartaxes decided it was time for humanity to throw off the yoke of it’s oppressors. Too long have their ways been subverted for those of their sun elf ‘liberators’. Human mages are flocking to his new court, worshippers of Surakel and the Old Sun Cult are coming out of hiding to spread the good word of the people. With his rebellion successful in the wake of the goblins’ conquest of Bahar, it is now to time to solidify his position and push back against the elves of Birzartanšes.

The harpies to the east and goblins to the west will be unpredictable threats while the Sun Elven nations to their north and south will be trying to subjugate them at any cost, lest the other human nations get the same idea. King Dartaxes will have his work cut out for him, but his fledgling nation has great potential. Soon, the Bulwari will lead Bulwar!

F25.title: Sareyand, the Ashen Phoenix
F25.lore:
Recent History
The kingdom of Sareyand is one of the Sun Elven kingdoms which remain in Bulwar after the Jexisian Civil War. The Sun Elves of Sareyand are the firmest believers that humans should always be under Sun Elves and they make sure their subjects understand their superiority, making them work their grand countryside villas and estates, the production going straight into Sun Elven coffers. Under King Eledas II Sarelzuir, the kingdom of Sareyand controlled the Upper Suran river valley and had a whole network of human vassals states which fed its armies with troops and its coffers with wealth. Since coming to power, his son, Eledas III, has lost control over much of that land due to constant gnollish invasions from the Salahad.

The harpies to the north still pose a threat to the Sun Elves, though the kingdom of Varamhar stands in the way of a concentrated push from the harpies into Sareyand, while the gnolls of Zokka to the south pose a much more urgent threat. With the Upper Suran and desert borders under their control, Sareyand’s entire southern border is at risk. To the west, the human city states cluster around the river valleys, paying homage to their Sun Elf masters or tribute to their gnollish oppressors. With the other Sun Elven monarchs each trying to seize power for themselves and fighting enemies on all sides, Eledas III is in a unique position to strike out from the relative security provided by a border with the Serpentspine. After the gnolls are dealt with, there will only be human cities between them and regional domination. The Ashen Phoenix shall rise.

F27.title: The Daughters of Firanya
F27.lore:
Recent History
The harpies of the Harpy Hills have been in Bulwar since time immemorial. Calling themselves the Daughters of Firanya, the first harpy and first and last monarch of the ancient Queendom, the harpies of the Harpy Hills believe themselves superior to the other wayward flocks to the east as they still remain in their ancestral homeland, Firanyalen on Jorkad Lake. Their current chieftess Ištara I Ayarato claims descendancy from Firanya herself and wishes to strengthen Ayarallen and reforge the ancient Harpy Queendom of her predecessor.

The situation for the Flock of Ayarallen is precarious, being considered a monstrous race by their neighbours means that they are ever met with resistance and hatred, unlike their more ‘refined’ sisters to the east who have allied themselves so closely with the Jaddari. The Sun Elves are always trying to push them out of Bulwar, back towards the mountains, and the goblins who have poured out into Bahar press at them from the west, seeing them as easier targets than the fortified Sun Elven monarchies. But harpies are hunters, they take what they want! And take they shall.

F28.title: Tluukt’s Pack
F28.lore:
Recent History
Gnollish kind crossed the Salahad long ago to raid and conquer Bulwar, forming mighty kingdoms that thrived on the Bulwari humans for centuries. While their rule was always shifting due to their warlike nature, it was not until Jaher landed and pushed them out of Bulwar with his mighty Sun Elf legions. Since those days, the gnolls have slowly made their way back into the Šad Sur, resettling it in disparate packs and clans. Tluukt saw an opportunity in uniting these disparate groups into one mighty pack, her own. She wishes to use this unity and the Bulwari’s complacency to strike deep into the human lands and restore the gnollish kingdoms of days gone by. Tluukt foresees a great future for the gnolls!

F29.title: Zokka’s Pack
F29.lore:
Recent History
Gnollish kind crossed the Salahad long ago to raid and conquer Bulwar, forming mighty kingdoms that thrived on the Bulwari humans for centuries. While their rule was always shifting due to their warlike nature, it was not until Jaher landed and pushed them out of Bulwar with his mighty Sun Elf legions. Since those days, the gnolls have been forced to live on the edges of the desert, a handful of fragmented packs with no real power or position to strike back. While Tluukt was consolidating power in Bulwar, Zokka had other plans. He and his pack pushed northeast into the kingdoms of the Upper Suran River. He felled Hašr and pushed Sareyand and Azka-Sur back towards their respective capitals, killing the last Sun Elven king of Azka-Sur in the process. Through his conquests Zokka became known as the Suneater and now seeks to devour all sources of Surael’s Light in Bulwar once and for all.

F34.title: Azka-Evran, the Teal Phoenix
F34.lore:
Recent History
Baharkand was once a great Sun Elven kingdom in Bahar. It controlled lands from the outskirts of Ovdal Tûngr to the source of Saluraš. When the goblins poured out of the mountains and attacked during the Greentide they managed to whittle away at Baharkand until they were able to take Aqatbar, the capital city. During the siege of Aqatbar, the king of Baharkand, Kaltan III Eletarzuir and his sons all fell in battle, leaving the kingdom, technically, heirless. With the Marblehead goblins now splitting the kingdom’s lands in two, the holdout at Azka-Evran could no longer claim the title of kingdom of Baharkand. But there is still hope. The last remaining possible heir, Deggarion I Evranzuir, a bastard of the late king, was commanding the garrison at Azka-Evran when the capital fell. Though he is unskilled in matters of rulership, he is an accomplished officer of the famous Wall Guard of Azka-Evran and has a keen military mind. Will he be able to reunite the kingdom and prove his worth and restore the glory of Baharkand?

F37.title: Irrliam, the Golden Phoenix
F37.lore:
Recent History
The kingdom of Irrliam is ruled by Taelarios I Irrliazuir. It was Taelarios who took the scattered cults and beliefs that remained in the wake of Jexis’ assassination and united them into the New Sun Cult. Through diplomacy, he was able to convince the reigning Sun Elven monarchs and various powerful human merchants to accept that Jaher’s divinity passed on to his kin upon his death, making all Sun Elves the Chosen of Surael. The diplomatic approach has served him well since the Treaty of Brasan guaranteed the rights of humans in his realm and vassal states and allowed the united New Sun Cult to be centered around him and Irrliam.

Thoroughly in his twilight years now, Taelarios’ is serving as regent for his grandson after the death of his eldest daughter in favour of whom he abdicated. But his time on Halann is coming to an end, he has grown old. He will have to get the affairs of his nation in order before his passing sparks an inevitable wave of confusion throughout the New Sun Cult. But with the gnolls pressing further into Bulwar from the Šad Sur to the south and the harpies to the north an ever-present threat, Irrliam has little room to breath and their human subjects are beginning to question the strength of their overlord.

F42.title: Varamhar, the White Phoenix
F42.lore:
Recent History
Varamhar, the youngest Sun Elven kingdom having risen only during the second Cinder War, sits on the borders of the Harpy Hills and has always had to deal with the harpies since the days of Jaher. They have had many innovative ideas to deal with the constant threats, such as the White Phoenix Guard or their roofed cities keeping away the worst of the sudden raids. Varamhar also contains the famous Varamhar Academy in Ulmiš Idiqlat. The academy is home to the brightest minds in Bulwar and some of the most powerful mages.

King Carodir I Varamzuir will have to deal with harpy threat to the north and the ever-encroaching gnollish incursion to the south in order to secure their borders. Once that is done, he can begin putting into motion his plans to prove the greatest magical hypothesis in known history. The White Phoenix will be ascendant.

F46.title: The Desert Legions of the Jadd
F46.lore:
Recent History
The concept of the Jadd was formed by Jaddar, the son of Jexis. When the second Phoenix Empire fell, Jaddar went with most of the Phoenix Legion east along the Golden Highway to the Far Salahad, being considered too loyal to the old empire to be reintegrated into the remaining Sun Elven states. With the help of the local Mašnsih people the legions adapted their tactics to their new desert home and reorganized themselves into the Desert Legions, a military state led by their commander Jaddar. They follow his teachings that all races must assist in fighting the Darkness and that no race should lead over another as Surael’s Light can shine through any and all who accept it.

With the Siadúnan Harpies to the north and east, the Harimraj in Rahen to the east and the gnolls to the west, the threat on all sides is daunting, but Jaddar’s legions are strong and empowered by the Light of Surael, nothing can stand in their way in their fight against the Darkness.

F50.title: Mulén, the Desert Sparrow
F50.lore:
Recent History
The Sundering of Siadanlén spelled the end of the harpy empire of the Far Salahad at the hands of Jaher, scattering the unity of the flocks. But with the collapse of the Phoenix Empire and the ensuing power vacuum, the Siadúnan flocks now act to crush the ambitions of their rival sisters so that their own may be realized.

Formed from the descendants and followers of prophetess Múla Swift-Sparrow, the Mulén flock seeks to enact her philosophy of learning from the wisdom of others. Pushed against the northern mountains and diplomatically isolated, they have found this impossible, and chafe against their confinement. From high in the mountains, the peerless enchantress Matriarch Kiríja Muló sees further than all, and spies opportunity in the coming war between Zokka and Jaddar that would also draw in the Siádan and Elaiénna flocks. With all its enemies set against one another, Mulén cannot allow this window of opportunity to close. Break free from the Far Salahad to see the world beyond, hear its words, and make your will known.

U05.title: Elizna, Masters of the Sea
U05.lore:
Recent History
Elizna is a kingdom of mixed cultures. The Brasanni and the Kheteratans make up the population and the Sun Elves rule over them from the island of Elizna. The subtle merging of Brasanni, Sun Elven, and Kheteratan cultures has meant that other Sun Elf kingdoms look on Elizna with disdain and Elizna looks back at them as pretenders, all claiming the right to lead the Phoenix Empire themselves as though they were Jaher’s equals. After the Jexisian Civil War the kingdom of Elizna was founded by the governor of Kheterata, Vulzin the Red, who amassed as much of the Phoenix Fleet in Elizna as he could in support of Elizar the Blooded, bastard son of Jaher and the rightful successor of Jexis.
Vulzin's daughter, Zaleria Vulzinzuir, would go on to secure much of northeastern Kheterata and would establish herself as a virtuous ruler in the eyes of many Kheteratans, especially the merchants and artisans who were ignored by the noble elites of Kheterata. Though this would cost her the support of many elven supremacists and empire loyalists, who would flee to Sareyand and the Jaddari Legion, her popularity with the Kheteratan middle class would secure Elizna's position there. In 1440AA, her son, Oloris Vulzinzuir, would succeed her and style himself as friend to all. However, with the division between Khetist and Sun Cultist growing and splitting the country’s attention, the kingdom may no longer be able to maintain its cultural dualism.

M01.title: Grizehûd, the Friendliest of Trolls.
M01.lore:
The trolls of what would eventually become Grizehûd started out as an ancient legion, marching into the Serpentspine to fight the armies of Aul-Dwarov. Over time, their organization was broken, and they scattered across the Serpentspine. The trolls dispersed into separate tribes, often no more than a dozen in number, but eventually the tribe of Grizehûd became one of the largest in the region following conquests of King Grize, who ruled with an iron fist.

When he died, he left the leadership of the tribe to his son, Grend Grizeson, who continued ruling as Grize had – savagely and with great cruelty. Brok, a troll who was outcast from the tribe, opposed him, and murdered him in a fit of rage. This earned him legitimacy as the third leader of the tribe of Grizehûd, which did not please him at all. He mentored the only surviving offspring of Grend, a young troll named Lagok, and taught all trolls of Grizehûd the ways of friendship and compassion. Then, one day, he simply vanished into the surrounding caves, never to be heard from again.

Now, the reins of power have been left to young Lagok, and the realm once again needs Brok’s wisdom. They will attempt to follow his teachings as best as they can, but whether they will stay true to them, only time will tell.

Z55.title: Clan Darkscale, Scattered but not Broken
Z55.lore:
The Darkscale clan has been in a period of decline for some time: once rulers of Khugdihr, now nothing but a shattered remnant hiding deep in the caverns of the Serpentspine.

After their emergence following the Dragonwake a millennia prior, the clan built a grand hoard of gold to their God and Master Nimrith the Red in occupied Khugdihr. This period of strength was brought to a crashing end with the foul murder of Nimrith by an army of adventurers in 1103.

Driven from their home of over six centuries by these adventurers, the Darkscales were believed by many to be shattered entirely for over two centuries, until a mage ruler of a minor clan gathered them once more to reclaim Khugdihr. The siege lasted nine whole years, until tragedy once more struck the Darkscale Kobolds with the death of their unifier to a stray crossbow bolt.

Following this defeat, the reclamation fragmented, and many clans dispersed deep into the caverns of the western Dwarovar. The recent orcish exodus following the marshalling of the Greentide under Korgus Dookanson did not spare them either, forcing them out of the larger tunnels into more twisted and sparse caverns where orcs cannot reach easily.

The current ruler Zorn Darkscale has managed to gather a few small clans into a cohesive entity, but survival is far from certain - after a recent disastrous clash with Clan Siegebreaker, the Darkscale's position is precarious indeed.

U01.title: Kheterata, the First Kingdom of Humanity
U01.lore:
Primer
On the banks of the Mother’s Sorrow lies the eldest of all human civilisations: Kheterata. It is a land dominated by the legacy of its divine founder Elikhet and his children, his cat-headed magical beings known as the Khet. Few mortals on this planet could even hope to reach the incredible power of the Khet, whose dominance is curtailed only by their own laziness and their inability to leave the banks of the great river from which they drew their strength. Created by Elikhet at the height of his power and invested with much of his divine might, the Sorrow is the center of Kheteratan civilization - a bountiful, fertile heartland protected from all foes by miles upon miles of desert to the east and west. Despite being protected by both the Khet and the vast Salahad Desert, the Kheteratan people have not always been safe from invasion.

Multiple times in Kheteratan history, the Khetarch, the leader of the Khet and head of the Khetist faith, would hand over the title of Steward of the Sorrow to an invader. There was the Damerian Republic invasion of 106 BA, whose occupation was only thwarted when Elikhet himself awoke from his tomb to drive them out. Or when Jaher’s daughter Jexis led her Phoenix Empire legions into Kheterat and the crown was handed over to the sun elf queen, until she was assassinated in Anbenncost in 1162. On other occasions, the Khet were powerless in the face of an even more powerful being they could not have defeated even if they tried. During the Dragonwake, the great bronze dragon Aakhet ate the ruling Khetarch and his family, established a puppet Khetarch, and led the forces of Kheterata on a grand conquest across Bulwar.

But the greatest foe of the Kheteratan people has always been the gnolls. From the infamous slaver Krah, to the brutal destruction by Rakhor, and finally the First Xhazobine who reportedly slew Elikhet in battle to establish gnollish control of the Sorrow, ancient Kheteratan history was dominated by their constant battles with the gnolls on both sides of the Salahad.

Recent History
After Aakhet met his alleged end at the hands of the Sand Demon of the Salahad, the Kheteratan crown was handed to a most unexpected man: King Sedoric Crodamos, an Alenic refugee turned mercenary king of Akasik. His dynasty would reign over Kheterata for centuries, regaining control after the death of Jexis.

Yet in recent years it became clear the great Empire spanning Koroshesh to Horashesh was collapsing from the inside. Gnollish raids had become more frequent. Religious instability was starting to divide the nation as different interpretations of the teachings of Elikhet were beginning to grip the Kheteratan people. When Grizaka Emperess-of-Gnollkind invaded the southern Sorrow, she was pushing on an open door. It did not take much for the empire to crack.

In 1439, Kheterata shattered. The Tijarikheti people who lived in the mountains at the source of the Sorrow scrambled into independent entities to protect themselves. Elizna forces walked into Koroshesh to establish control of the great city on the Divenhal. Civil war broke out in Kheterata between the temple of Nirat and those still loyal to Kheterata. And to its south stood the hordes of Gnollakaz, led by the self-proclaimed Empress-Of-Gnollkind, ready to end the oldest human empire once and for all.

U07.title: Viakkoc, the Scourge of All Seas
U07.lore:
Primer
The Seascourge Gnolls are a relatively new people, born from the remnants of the Most High Kingdom of Viakkoc that survived it’s disastrous war with Gnollakaz over who got to invade Kheterata. Despite their newness, they’ve rapidly distinguished themselves from other gnolls by fully adopting the corsair lifestyle, with Cannor facing raids and piracy on a level unseen since the Era of Black Ice.

Under the dominance of the Seascourge gnolls, the Khasa people who once dominated what is now Viakkoc still survive in pockets, and it is known that a Prince of Khasa fled east and now seeks to save his people - though whether he means the Khasani specifically, or all of Akasik, is hotly debated.

Recent History
With their loss in the War for the Sorrow, the Viakkoca gnolls fled northwards into Akasik and established their current Grand Corsair Kingdom, destroying the Kingdom of Khasa while establishing the Republic of Ekha as a tributary. The Kingdom of Deshak has survived by declaring fealty to the King of Eborthil, establishing itself as Viakkoc’s foremost rival, while also hosting the final surviving member of the Khasani royal family.

Beyond its rivalry with the Kingdoms of Eborthil and Deshak, the Grand Corsair Kingdom has also sets its sights northwards; rather than launching another futile war against Gnollakaz, Viakkoc X declared his ambition to conquer the West Diven and Dameshead as the ‘Scourge of All Seas’. His son, Viakkoc XI, would adopt this as a new dynastic name and continue this ambition.

U18.title: The Free Republic of Re’uyel
U18.lore:
Recent History
The Republic of Re’uyel was not always a republic and was not always so well known for its freedoms. Until recently, the nation was ruled by an elective monarchy which was deemed incompetent and overthrown by a group of human and Sun Elven lower nobility during the Decades of Devastation, replacing it with the republic which stands today and banning all forms of slavery and indentured servitude. This change in government coupled with the recent succession wars with the kingdom of Baharkand meant that the people of Re’uyel have a long way to go before their nation is stabilized.

More recently, there have been conflicts of faith in the republic, as the recent events have led to a decrease in faith in the Chosen Ones. Secret societies have started preaching the ways of the Old Sun Cult in your capital, and Jadd missionaries have been seen spreading their young faith in the countryside, but wherever these faiths have crossed paths elsewhere in Bulwar, trouble has followed.

U20.title: Azka-Sur, the Fortress of the Sun
U20.lore:
Recent History
The history of Azka-Sur is as old as Bulwar, built by Genies, nurtured by dwarves, conquerors of the Suran River. Their kingdom eventually declined over the centuries and had been almost overrun by gnolls when Jaher landed and proved himself to be Surael reborn, forcing the gnolls back to the desert from whence they came. Since those days they have been devout followers of the Cult, not allowing their ways or their Chosen to be corrupted by power and decadence. Now, however, it sits at its weakest point. When Zokka’s pack came across the Salahad during the last gnoll invasion and wreaked havoc along the Suran River, Azka-Sur lost lands, allies, and their last Sun Elven king who had no heir. Now, Azka-Sur is ruled by a human and has been pushed back into the valley surrounding the fortress. With only their dwarven allies in Seghdihr, they are beset by an endless sea of evil gnolls and heretical Jaddists with no likelihood of help from the Chosen to the west whose corruption, it is well known, they hold in great disdain.

Y86.title: Azkare, The Dayspring
Y86.lore:
Primer

It is certain that, in the coming years, the people of Haless’ desire for independence and their desire for peace will meet with terrible hostility. In the inevitable conflict, we must unite, or be drowned in blood.
- Hiderion Tonanzuir, speaking to his troops before the Battle of Lektonmai, 1441

Primer
Standing resolute against the rumbling powers at their borders, the Kai townships have always been a picture of provincial simplicity. Their stalwart republican traditions and fearsome guerilla tactics have guaranteed independence since the death of Jaerel, the last great unifier of Haless and the collapse of the eastern Phoenix Empire. Since its collapse they have yet to rise to any real importance in regional politics, instead acting as a series of buffer states between the Tamran Empire to the south and the Xia to their north.

Unlike many of their neighbours, the Kai harboured no particular animosity towards the elves left in the wake of the Phoenix Legion’s hasty retreat from Haless, instead integrating them into their republics. The largest of these fledgling communities is in Azkare’s own Citadel of the Dawn, an old Jaherian stronghold still occupied by the descendants of his soldiers, and it is from this stock of elves that an unlikely figure would rise to become lord of Azkare.

Recent Events
In 1438, Hiderion Tonanzuir, a Sunrise Elf who had faithfully served several generations of Azkare lords, was elected as lord of Azkare, the smallest of the four townships. An elf of unending ambition, he began to reform nearly every aspect of the state, culminating in 1441 when the newly formed Shining Lancers, a legion of elite elven cavalry tracing their roots back to Jaher’s legion, soundly defeated the combined armies of Khao Elnak and Lo Ngoen in the Battle of Lektonmai.

Hiderions ambition did not end on that battlefield. He returns home to Azkare with renewed fervour, dreaming of unity. With the Tamran to the south in the midst of a brutal civil war which transcends the boundaries of mortality, and the Xia to the north threatened by a renewed push from the Hobgoblin Command, there has been no better time for resurgent elven rule. Will Azkare remain a final footnote in the history of elven rule on the continent, or will they blossom anew to build a final, peaceful, Halessi empire?

Y60.title: Lo Ngoen, The Silver Shields
Y60.lore:
Primer
Standing resolute against the rumbling powers at their borders, the Kai townships have always been a picture of provincial simplicity. Their stalwart republican traditions and fearsome guerilla tactics have guaranteed independence since the death of Jaerel, the last great unifier of Haless and the collapse of the eastern Phoenix Empire. Since its collapse they have yet to rise to any real importance in regional politics, instead acting as a series of buffer states between the Tamran Empire to the south and the Xia to their north.

Unlike many of their neighbours, the Kai harboured no particular animosity towards the elves left in the wake of the Phoenix Legion’s hasty retreat from Haless, instead integrating them into their republics. The largest of these fledgling communities is in Azkare’s own Citadel of the Dawn, an old Jaherian stronghold still occupied by the descendants of his soldiers, and it is from this stock of elves that an unlikely figure would rise to become lord of Azkare.

Recent Events
In 1441, Hiderion Tonanzuir gave us a terrible defeat at Lektonmai. His novel Shining Lancers overwhelmed our infantry after a devastating flank attack that killed both our and Khao Elnak’s lord. It was only when young Rama Buakae picked up the Lord’s standard right as it was about to fall with its owner that a complete disaster was averted, one that would have perhaps spelled the end of Lo Ngoen there and then.

With the past lord’s death and Lektonmai lost, it was time to elect a new Lord of Lo Ngoen. The elections were fierce, but in the end, the choice landed on Rama. As a ruler elected more for his fame and passionate revanchism than for his merit, he will have to prove his worth to his township and show that the Silver Shields’s fame across Haless is worth more than few elven horsemen.

Y59.title: Thirabnir, the Eternal Plains
Y59.lore:
Primer
Standing resolute against the rumbling powers at their borders, the Kai townships have always been a picture of provincial simplicity. Their stalwart republican traditions and fearsome guerilla tactics have guaranteed independence since the death of Jaerel, the last great unifier of Haless and the collapse of the eastern Phoenix Empire. Since its collapse they have yet to rise to any real importance in regional politics, instead acting as a series of buffer states between the Tamran Empire to the south and the Xia to their north.

Unlike many of their neighbours, the Kai harboured no particular animosity towards the elves left in the wake of the Phoenix Legion’s hasty retreat from Haless, instead integrating them into their republics. The largest of these fledgling communities is in Azkare’s own Citadel of the Dawn, an old Jaherian stronghold still occupied by the descendants of his soldiers, and it is from this stock of elves that an unlikely figure would rise to become lord of Azkare.

Recent Events
While across the borders a war is raging between the townships, Thirabnir is enjoying a minor Golden Age under the long reign of Narai Chaiya. A man feared by his neighbours and loved by his citizens, he has guided his country for 44 years now, carefully balancing relations between the dwarves of Verkal Ozovar and the now Ghost-Emperor of Baihon Xinh, while simultaneously repelling threats from the tribalistic Rongbek and from instigators inside the Township. 

He has reformed substantial parts of the state, introducing new laws that have brought many people out of poverty, while cultivating literary and social culture in Haiban, The City of Adventurers. 
Having almost reached his eighties, the Lord of Thirabnir looks now to cement his legacy, hoping to pass his last reforms and prepare the township to withstand the ambitions of the many grasping lords who begin to unfold their plans in Thidinkai. 

The political situation is more tense than ever and only time will tell whether the next Lord will stand up to the legacy of his great predecessor, or whether these will be the last years of an independent Thirabnir.

Y62.title: Khao Elnak, the Sky Runners
Y62.lore:
Primer
Standing resolute against the rumbling powers at their borders, the Kai townships have always been a picture of provincial simplicity. Their stalwart republican traditions and fearsome guerilla tactics have guaranteed independence since the death of Jaerel, the last great unifier of Haless and the collapse of the eastern Phoenix Empire. Since its collapse they have yet to rise to any real importance in regional politics, instead acting as a series of buffer states between the Tamran Empire to the south and the Xia to their north.

Unlike many of their neighbours, the Kai harboured no particular animosity towards the elves left in the wake of the Phoenix Legion’s hasty retreat from Haless, instead integrating them into their republics. The largest of these fledgling communities is in Azkare’s own Citadel of the Dawn, an old Jaherian stronghold still occupied by the descendants of his soldiers, and it is from this stock of elves that an unlikely figure would rise to become lord of Azkare.

Recent Events
In 1441, Hiderion Tonanzuir gave us a terrible defeat at Lektonmai. His novel Shining Lancers overwhelmed our infantry after a devastating flank attack that killed both our and Lo Ngoen’s lord. While the outcome of the battle was decisive, it would take more to subjugate both of the townships completely. 

Mya Saetae presented great diplomatic ability during the negotiations, securing a land corridor between Khao Elnak and our allies in Lo Ngoen, while somehow minimising the monetary reparations due to Azkare. Based on that, and her connections to one of the Dauloph families of Lot Dekkhang, she quickly rose to power as the lord of Khao Elnak. Now, many opportunities and threats lie ahead of her. Many rulers and lords attracted by her beauty and possessions will send emissaries her way along with gifts and compliments, looking to make Khao Elnak dependent on them. 

But be careful, future lovers, Mya Saetae knows how to wrap someone around her fingers - and with the blood of the savvy Saetae family in her veins, she will not give up so easily on her ambitions...

R51.title: The Lotus Throne of the Second Harimraj
R51.lore:
'And on this day I claim the title that has lain fallow for half a millennium. With the crowns of a dozen kingdoms set at my feet I declare the rebirth of the Harimraj. The Lotus Court will bloom once more, and by our honor never wilt again.'
-Ramapalar I 'The Reunifier', upon crowning himself Harimraja, 1305 AA

Primer
Coloquially referred to as Dhenijanraj, the Second Harimraj is the largest political entity in Haless. The harimari of Dhenijanraj rule over half of the Raheni subcontinent from the Golden Palace in Dhenijansar, their domain stretching from the Shenral Hills in the south to the confluence of the Narajandi and Kharunyana Rivers in the north. This expanse contains multiple human ethnicities, such as the Ghavaanaj and Ghankedhen, living alongside the tigerlike harimari as part of the ancient hierarchy of the Raheni Caste System.

While the Caste System may be ancient, the Dhenijanraj is not - the original Harimraj disintegrated in 721 AA, and Rahen was divided between feuding warlords and petty kingdoms for centuries. It was only in 1305 AA that Ramapalar Lotus Claw of Durasidheni succeeded in unifying the Dhenbasana River basin. Ramapalar crowned himself Harimraja and set about rebuilding the old empire - he founded a bureaucracy called the Raj Ministries to govern his vast holdings and devolved local power to the Prabhia (Landholders) and Senaptia (Warlords) to ease administration of the empire. Though his conquests stalled against the merchant cities in the south and the Ruin Kingdoms to the north, Ramapalar's efforts stabilized Rahen and built a unified state for the first time in centuries.

Recent History
Since the time of Ramapalar, however, Dhenijanraj has been in a slow decline. The Great Slave Revolt of 1355 saw the breakaway of the Republic of Bhuvauri, who hold the Kharunyana Delta to this day. Disinterested Rajas elevated a Grand Vizier from among their vassals to administer the realm in their stead, and delegated ever more power to the Ministries and Prabhia. Civil Wars and the Smothering Sickness of 1414 would devastate the countryside in the early 1400s and leave its infrastructure crumbling. The current Raja, Indranayar, has reigned for 23 years, during which time corruption has only grown while the power and prestige of the Raja has decayed. Despite the efforts of the current Grand Vizier, Manava of Rabhidaraj, Dhenijanraj teeters on the edge of disaster, particularly as the hobgoblin Command crashes down upon Rahen from the north. Whether the fall of the Second Harimraj can be averted, and the honor of the Lotus Throne saved, is uncertain. Only a herculean effort in the coming turbulent years may yet bring glory back to the Lotus Claw dynasty and their empire.

R62.title: The Command: Scourge of Haless
R62.lore:
"The War Room is in agreement. The Great Campaign shall now commence."
 -Grand Marshal Moguwon Wolfborn, on conclusion of the Kenu-Ike held 1430

Recent History
That was then, this is now. The hobgoblins of North Rahen have united for the first time in history not under the personal power of a mage, but under a larger political system. The Confederation of Commands, signed in blood in 1392, binds the Boar, Wolf, and Lion Commands together under a single leader known as the Grand Marshal. Consensus between the oft-warring tribes is achieved through an institution known as the Kenu-Ike, or Settlement of Swords, less literally translated as simply The War Room. Although recent, this system of governance has already seen its first victories: under the rule of Grand Marshal Moguwon Wolfborn, the Great Command seized the opportunity provided by the withering Alliance of the Heroic Realms to conquer vast swaths of Shamakhad.

To the south, the decadent Second Harimraj slips further into its slow decline. To the west, the remaining Ruin Kingdoms scramble furiously to muster what strength they have left into an army capable of holding back the hobgoblin threat. To the east, the Oni of the Demon Hills brood, and the warrior-monks of the Xia find themselves in a panic, believing that our Bloodsong slaves' recent resettlement to their borders signals a coming assault. They are not mistaken.

The army of the Great Command is, at this present moment, the strongest in all of Haless—perhaps even all of Halann. It is a sharpened sword, every part of it honed to a keening edge; whichever direction it is pointed in shall bleed. And it is the sole choice of the Grand Marshal, at the consensus of the Kenu-Ike, in which direction this blade shall cut. Winning, however, is only the first step—governing comes next. The Hobgoblin people must reform out of their tribal roots and become a society of blood and iron, one capable of claiming all of Haless and holding it within their ironclad grip.

R38.title: The Free Land of Bhuvauri
R38.lore:
“Hah! ‘Free Land.’ What mockery of freedom and language.” -Salar of the Lucid String, Philosopher from the School of the Radiant Sun

Primer
The slave state of Bhuvauri rules from its capital Sramaya, the major port at the mouth of the Kharunyana River and one of the most prominent trade channels in all of Rahen. Three families share power and act as political blocs, yet despite factional squabblings, all these fictive kin know that perpetuating slavery is paramount to their power. Through indoctrinating and training enslaved children, Bhuvauri maintains the Adasa, a military and bureaucrat caste with no loyalties save the state.

Practiced in Rahen since written record, slavery has existed as a minor institution reserved for violent criminals and prisoners of war. When bound, these slaves were guaranteed rights (though such guarantees were inconsistent) such as adequate pay, days of rest, the right to worship, and a contractual duration for enslavement.

Yet in the wake of the Phoenix Empire’s collapse in the 12th century AA, Rahen’s population declined, and as bloodthirsty warlords became administrative landlords, they sought to fill labor shortages with a new, far crueler version of an already ugly system: chattel slavery. Relying on East Sarhalian networks of gnoll and Mengi slavers, Rahen’s slave population ballooned, driven by cash crop profits and cruelly indifferent rulers.

All this came to a head with the Bhuvauri Revolt of the 1350s which saw disunited slaves united into a “Free Land”, or Bhuvauri in Dhujati. After its renowned victory over the Second Harimraj at the Battle of Tanyatta, Bhuvauri secured independence and liberty... for some. The slave rebels-turned-rulers, fearful of their non-slave populace, continued to trade in human lives by founding the Adasa to perpetuate their might.

Recent History
With the Harimraj distracted by the inept Raja Indranayar and the Command’s lightning conquest of the Ruin Kingdoms, Bhuvauri sensed opportunity. After years of infiltration and preparation, the Slave Republic marched on their old rival Bim Lau in 1443. Bhuvauri won several surprise naval victories along Reanthung’s islets before campaigning up the Telebei River, and after a swift siege, the citadel at Bieng Phang fell, securing valuable Rahen coast and checking Bim Lau’s ambition to reforge the Empire of the Bom. But as Bhuvauri established themselves in their new territories, the Great Isudhen Typhoon struck, destroying warships, merchant fleets, fortifications, and washing away entire settlements: The storm killed over 300,000, devastating the region for generations and hardening Bhuvauri’s slave-focused strategies.

Yet despite their advances, Bhuvauri still struggles to rule over their native populace. Seeking to tighten their precarious grasp on power, the Free Land casts their gaze across the Gulf of Rahen, pining for fresh slave markets; Only by expanding the Adasa can Bhuvauri hope to overcome its administrative shortcomings.

Y01.title: The Tagharoghi of Azjakuma
Y01.lore:
Primer
The Oni are a presence eternal. The oldest nation in Halann, Azjakuma was founded by disparate clans of ogres united in their regret of betraying their ancient masters, the True Giants. In the rugged cliffs of Haless, these pariahs have forged a hermit kingdom that has stood for centuries as ogres and trolls were hunted to death. Shunned and reviled by all of Haless, the Oni have grown into a unique culture, their very forms warped by the demonic energies they have harnessed to survive.
Their society is organised around the four Shirgii, ancient fortresses home to castes of wise scholars, fierce warriors, cunning rogues and wealthy tyrants. While outsiders see them as monstrous, the Oni culture holds tradition, arts and knowledge in high esteem - the cunning trickster, the serene monk, and the wise mystic are held in the same regard as the ferocious warrior. The Oni are stewards of immense knowledge and teachings passed down over their aeons of existence. Skilled crafters and mages, many esoteric techniques originate with them such as the creation of Korashi, known as Black Damestear.

Recent Events:
 The current Rashenbir, Tiraga Aromo, is a proud traditionalist from Shinukhorchi, the oldest and greatest Shirgii. Under his rule the Oni have turned inward, withdrawing from the world at large and scaling back their antagonism of their neighbours. His rule has been a stable and prosperous one, yet the world has not been static. The ambitious heir, Akira Surgoshi, resents this isolation and has greater ambitions for the Oni and Haless…
To the east, the Shuvuushudi tribes, once scattered and disorganised, have united under the leadership of a dreaded warlord known only as the Faceless Queen. The Rashenbir scoffs at the notion of sheepherders threatening the mighty Oni, but keeps an eye trained towards the steppe.
In the south, the tyrant lords of Bianfang consolidate their grip over southern Yanshen and begin to dream of an empire. While humans are soft, short-lived and weak, they must be kept in check.
Above all, the rising Hobgoblin Command in the west casts a shadow over the entire continent. This organised army is no mere horde, but a unique threat entirely. There is no doubt of their ambition to subjugate all of Haless, but perhaps it need not come to violence. We are both hated and feared by the world at large, so perhaps an accord can be struck - if nothing else, to grant us respite to ready ourselves for war.
The world is changing, and the Oni cannot afford to sit idle in their mountain thrones - cunning diplomacy, skilled planning, and brutal force will be needed to navigate the storm ahead. Come what may, the walls of the Shirgii have withstood all oncomers, and the Oni must remain a presence eternal.

Y09.title: Bianfang, the Stormcloud of the West
Y09.lore:
Seated in the fortress city of Bianfang, the Border Lords have always proclaimed themselves to be righteous guardians of all Yanshen against the chaotic monks to the south and the monsters to the north. The pampered eunuch rulers of the East have always regarded them as mere authoritarian brigands running a protection racket on the upper Yanhe.

In truth, centuries of constant skirmishing with the surrounding powers - and each other - have forged the Western Yansheni into a disciplined, highly regimented society. Everyone from the millet farmer to the decorated general understands their role in the rigid hierarchy. Those who break ranks are swiftly and brutally dealt with. The individualistic monks of Xianjie and the indolent eunuchs of Yanszin may decry this style of rule as tyrannical, but it has allowed the West to weather storms of war which would have toppled lesser realms. In recent decades, the border lords have indeed become the storm cloud themselves, led by King Xinzhu Long.

Born in 1403 to a lowly district prefect, Long proved himself a highly ambitious strategic prodigy at the young age of 20, when he took charge of a campaign against Wudikmen after the entire command staff was executed for treason, a sentence Long himself carried out. After a series of stunning victories, Bianfang annexed the Dikcha province in 1424 AA. Delighted with his wild success, the Duke promoted Long to Marshal, the youngest ever to hold that office. Long followed this triumph with campaigns against the Vermillion Throne of Zyujyut, taking the provinces of Tounaapyun and Jangeoigong in 1427, earning the epithet the White Dragon of the West after he set the Zyujyut forests aflame.

His star rose even further in 1430, when the childless Duke named Long heir as he lay on his deathbed. In his coronation address to the assembled Marshals of Bianfang, he articulated his vision for the realm. To fulfill its role as guardian of Yanshen, Bianfang must follow the example of Harimar and Jaher and unite Yanshen under one banner - one ruler, one law, one land. To allow Yanshen to be divided and misruled by the eunuchs, or outright conquered by the growing terror of the Command, was unacceptable. No mere Duke could accomplish this. With the thunderous applause of the Marshals, Xinzhu Long proclaimed himself King of Bianfang.

He quickly set about this great work with diplomatic guile and strategic brilliance. Later that year, he propped up the Xi Dynasty in the Xiyun Civil War, creating two weak opponents out of one strong one. In 1435 AA, he partitioned the city state of Chatzin with the Eternal Court of Jingqiu. He allied with Szicheng in a joint conquest of Lamwun in 1438, only to promptly crush their armies and seize both realms for Bianfang in 1439. Chanting ‘Storm! Storm! Storm!’ the King’s forces rout army after army in cascades of crossbow bolts and cannonfire.

As 1444 draws to a close, the Stormcloud of Bianfang is poised to roll over the land in all directions. From the Tiger Fort of Hubao to the Great Temples of Tianlou, all shall know the iron discipline and fearsome thunder of the White Dragon.

Y72.title: Chien Binhrung, Gem of Vimdatrong
Y72.lore:
Primer
Vimdatrong is on the brink of war. Dinh Caoban, the traitorous son of the infamous Ghost Emperor of Hon Sai, has split the realm of Baihon Xinh in two, raising his banners against his now ethereal father. The keen eyes of the desert raiders of Sirtan, as well as the Mountain King of Verkal Ozovar, view this conflict as an opportunity to achieve their own ambitions, while the pirates of Pinghoi and the Mercantile Kingdom of Nagon slink away from the rule of the Caoban dynasty.
