But this is not their story. On the eastern shorelines of Vimdatrong, two destined rivals fight in the same war for a different purpose. Kudet Kai, the so-called ‘Unkillable Kai’, once unjustly slew our Anroang (Queen) Duyen, and cast her body to the flames—a grave insult to our funerary customs. We were close to ending their disgraceful kingdom once, only for a lone band of Harimari to rout our sieging army and force us into a loathsome truce. Now, those same Harimari run the government of Kudet Kai and have sided with Dinh Caoban to ensure our downfall. This cannot stand! We will seize Kudet Kai’s lands, its people, and finally avenge our Anroang!

...But what then? The threats to our freedom will never cease. The Ghost Emperor desires a unified Thidinkai, and the desert raids won’t simply stop after the war. The only path to true freedom is through conquest. A fair subjugation, perhaps, one where everyone is treated fair regardless of custom or race, but subjugation nonetheless. Such a lofty ambition can not be accomplished easily. We will need all the help we can get, but who to trust? Not the snakes surrounding us, no, but perhaps the legendary Anroangs of old can aid our kingdom once again…

We are the Binhrung. Our Kingdom is Chien Binhrung. And by the grace of Anroang Nhitrac the Young, we will rule the peoples of Yanglam and Kai Alike.

Y93.title: Danggun, the Great Kingdom
Y93.lore:
Primer
According to local lore, the vast territory that would later be known as the Kingdom of Gowon was once a tapestry of warring tribes, ensnared in perpetual conflict both amongst themselves and with the enigmatic spirits that inhabited the region. Enter Manjang, a sage of profound wisdom and vision, who embarked on a transformative quest to usher in an era of peace, harmony, and prosperity. Under Manjang's wise rule, the disparate tribes coalesced into a unified realm, with him as the revered sage king.

Venturing to the mystical Isle of Nakseseom, Manjang negotiated a groundbreaking pact with spiritkind, a pact now known as the Compact which endures to this very day. Following the sage king's passing, his trusted advisor, a member of the once-potent Hwalbaek (Bowlord) clan, undertook a pilgrimage to the sacred island. There, he faced judgment, setting an enduring standard for future generations.

The Hwalbaek clan, though extinguished, left a permanent mark on Gowon, the name becoming synonymous with the word nobility in the annals of history. This golden age of prosperity, however, faced a tragic interruption on the fateful Day of the Ashen Skies. Barren lands and invading nomadic people from the west, known as the Shuvüüsh, heralded an era of devastation. Some spirits, sensing the opportunity, defied the Compact, but the valiant armies of Gowon, aided by the remaining loyal spirits, vanquished the traitorous entities in a fierce confrontation.

After decades of conflict, some of the inhabitants, battered by the relentless onslaught of the Shuvüüshudi, chose to flee to the southern mountains, birthing a new identity, the Giteuni. Others remained and sought refuge within colossal walled cities in the north. These monumental bastions eventually fell under Shuvüüsh occupation, leading to a cultural fusion that birthed the Kingdom of Guwamuud, under the rightful rule of King Bhengudak.

Fast forward to the year 1081, and the once-mighty Guwamuud crumbled, ushering in an era of intense power struggles and civil war. By the year 1140, three dominant forces had risen to prominence in the region following the conflict. In the northwest, the Oejeok, an amalgamation of Shuvüüshudi and native cultures, vied for supremacy. To the south, the Giteuni, torchbearers of ancient Gowon, clung to their heritage. In the east, the diplomatic and wealthy Wonju and their kingdom of Danggun schemed for power.

Recent History
The realm of Danggun, unified in 1394 under the leadership of Jeonghun Gyo, brought together the Oejeok, Giteuni and King's own people the Wonju through the artful deployment of both warfare and diplomacy. After he united the three kingdoms, the new King traveled to the isle of Nakseseom, where the spirits judged him worthy of the throne.

Jeonghun Gyo, in his quest for unity, orchestrated a matrimonial alliance with a Oejeok princess, weaving intricate connections between Danggun and the Dalmangaji kingdoms. Simultaneously, the heir to the throne, Haeng Gyo, was bound by betrothal to a princess of the Giteuni, to ensure the lasting loyalty of both kingdoms.

As of year 1444, Danggun thrives, yet internal division and external adversaries threaten to tear the kingdom apart. To the west, the Shuvüüshudi lurk in anticipation of the ailing king's demise, eager to reclaim what once slipped through their fingers. Meanwhile, the Oejeok and Giteuni grow restless, feeling their power waning over the years, waiting for the right opportunity to rise again.

Amidst these complexities, Haeng Gyo, oblivious to the brewing turmoil at home, embarks on a pilgrimage to Nakseseom. There, amidst ethereal spirits, his worthiness is subjected to judgment. The pivotal question resonates; Can Haeng Gyo navigate the looming challenges, or is the kingdom destined for destruction?

L55.title: Pirate Matriarchy of Naléni
L55.lore:
Primer
On the tip of the Great Horn lies the domain of the Nalénian harpies, dreaded pirates and feared sirens all. A rugged land, bordered on one side by the great Telekenid range and on the other by the salty waters of the Rahen Gulf, Naléni has made its presence felt on the waves through its rampant support for piracy. Indeed, the state itself is less of a true harpy matriarchy, and more of a hybrid confederation between one and a pirate republic. Pirate captains, known as Odanchesta in the Nalénian tongue, constantly vie for political power amongst themselves and against the Matriarchy - headed by the Nephiuéda dynasty - creating a fractious and discordant nation.

Harpies first came to the Telekenid mountain range following the fall of Siádenlen in the wake of Jaher’s conquests across Bulwar in the 11th century, settling in small groups throughout the region. Over the course of the next two centuries, the weakness of the Mengi kingdom of Irkan Eparta would allow the Nephiuéda dynasty (who had established themselves the dominant clan amongst the harpies of the area) to gradually encroach upon the kingdom, finally annexing it in its entirely in 1176 under the leadership of the first Nalénian matriarch, Muréda Nephiuéda.

Following this victory Muréda would negotiate an agreement with the Odanchesta to keep the realm stable, now known as the Muréda accord, wherein the Odanchesta would elect one from amongst their number to serve as Dread Captain, or Idar Odanchés - a kind of quasi-military ruler sharing power with the Matriarch. Despite this, the subsequent two centuries would see internal power struggles dominate Naléni’s political landscape, as well as the continued development of a strong seafaring culture. Ever more harpies took to a life on the water, finding great success pirating the rich gulf trade for both mates and plunder.

Recent history
The arrival of the Raheni colonisers to Madriamilak in the early 15th century would shake the state. As multiple Mengi realms along the coast fell to the Raheni the Matriarchy began grasping for internal power anew, fearing that their lands might be next. The independent Odanchesta however had no such concerns, seeing the dramatic increase in Raheni shipping as providing an incredible opportunity for their enrichment.

Unity in Naléni is currently maintained only by the recent election of the current Matriarch’s daughter, Bimogíre Nephiuéda, as Idar Odanchés, due both to the support of the Matriarch and her own prowess at the helm of a raiding ship. A rare moment of unity provides perhaps the chance to ensure Naléni’s survival…

Y97.title: The Second Empire of Baihon Xinh
Y97.lore:
Primer
The First Baihon Xinh – ‘Alliance of the Three-Soul Soldier’ – was a federation of the kingdoms Hon Sai, Mo Hoang and Nagon that came to dominate much of Vidamtrong between the years 760 AA and 1140 AA. A union of the Tamran, Phonan and Gon peoples, this first Baihon Xinh brought about a golden age for Thidinkai not seen since the Day of Ashen Skies.

The alliance would wax and wane in power and influence, but always stood together when threatened. This held true when Jaher’s son, Elizar the Blooded, led his legions to invade in 1080 AA. Defying expectations, the alliance’s well disciplined military and deployment of dwarven artillery shattered the invaders and saved Thidinkai from the elven yoke.

The alliance would not outlive Jaher by long, however; the king of Hon Sai led a coup and declared an empire with a single head, but he ultimately died without a successor. The following interregnum would see the rise of the soldiering Caoban family to the throne of Hon Sai.

Recent History
In the year 1385, Caoban Sang succeeded his father to the throne of Hon Sai. An ambitious man, he brought the warlord period to an end and in 1400 declared the Empire of Baihon Xinh reborn . Not content with the extent of the empire of old, he ultimately forced all of Thidinkai and much of Bomdan and the Lupulan to bow in tribute.

This golden age was cut short in 1433 when the Lupulan tribes rose in revolt against the vassal king of Jaya Raya. Aided by dark spirits and the jungle itself, the rebellion drained the armies and treasury of Baihon Xinh; an opportunity seized by the Yan pirate queen Ping in 1439 when she conquered the city of Cay Cam Sinh in a fortnight.

These setbacks are not something a brilliant man like Sang will let get in the way of his vision, but he lacks the time to see it through. He must rely on his successor, but there are rumors his sons plan to divide up his empire after his death. If only there were a way to surpass the limits of mortality, he could see it done himself…

Y65.title: Arawkelin, Bulwark of the Phoenix Empire
Y65.lore:
It is said that the Gataw are some of the world's best sailors, with many migrations leaving the Ringlet Isles in centuries past to settle in lands as far as Sarhal. The last Gataw migration, however, was different. The Great conqueror Hanto Hanto, hero of the Ringlet Isles, set his sights on the untamed Lupulan rainforest. With him went many of the people of the Ringlet Isles, eager for a life beyond simple subsistence. More still were driven to Hanto Hanto’s kingdom from the Ringlet Isles by a magical plague in 1000 AA. This exodus truly ensured the rise of Hanto Hanto’s realm, allowing them to found a prosperous city at the mouth of the Lupulan's greatest river. 

For a time, it seemed like this kingdom was to be the legacy Hanto Hanto left. This was disproven in the middle of the 11th century when the Sun King Jaher arrived, whose Phoenix Legions would reshape the lives of the Gataw again. It was during this time that the kingdom received the name it continues to wear proudly to this day. "Araw" meaning sun in Gataw, "kel" and "in" meaning harbour and river in Sun Elven. The king of the newly styled Arawkelin swore fealty to Jaher, seeing an opportunity to elevate the realm under his radiance. A great port was constructed at the river mouth, cementing Arawkelin as the great capital of the Kelino people. This harbour served as an intermediary port of call for Jaher’s navy, which would invade Yanshen in 1090 by way of the loyal Arawkelin. 

However, in 1127 this age of prosperity, sheltered under the wings of the phoenix, came to an end. Jaher was assassinated by the Yansheni he had subjugated, and within a decade, his son and heir Jaerel had his life cut short, too. What followed was a collapse of the Phoenix Empire in Haless, with many of the elves retreating west. Some, however, either due to being unable to flee or not wishing to, found a new home in Arawkelin. Fleeing warlords that rose to squabble over the ashes of their empire, they helped bolster Arawkelin when the rest of the continent faced turmoil. These elves trained the Kelino anticipating conflict with these warlords, but this never came to pass. Arawkelin was left with an elite army enabling their invasion of the Lupulan, eventually crushing the neighboring Tengalam of Lembingtana after their Padhahkan passed in 1271. 

In 1444 Arawkelin is beset by new challenges. Some Sun Elves stayed, and became part of Kelino society, forming the new aristocracy as a class of half-elves emerged, unique to Haless. Many, however, eventually left for Bulwar, or went north to Azkare. Though battle-hardened and seaworthy, Arawkelin is now set upon by the pirates of Pinghoi led by their pirate queen, Ping Shuang. If the city of the Sunharbour River is to reassert itself, it faces a dual challenge. It must wrestle control over the seas from the pirates who seized its fleet only scant years ago, and it must tame the Lupulan that it encroaches on and the Yanglam within. Whether Arawkelin will master these tests is yet to be seen.

H21.lore:
Eordand is a region of Northwestern Aelantir populated by Fey and Season worshipping Eordan Ruinborn Elves. It is centered around the Domandrod, an ancient and forbidden forest populated by the Fey, and around this axle the Eordan build civilizations like spokes on a wheel. The Druidic magic taught to the Eordan after the Day of the Ashen Skies broke the Eordan into five cultures: the Selpheregi who venerate Spring, the Caamas who venerate Summer, the Tuathak who venerate Autumn, the Snecboth who venerate Winter, and the Peitar who strive to maintain balance and heed the orders of their Fey masters.

Eordand is soaked in Druidic magical power from the Feyrealm's shadow over the Domandrod. The Eordan primarily dwell around the Domandrod, having left the custody of the Fey long ago, apart from the few chosen Gladewardens. Eordand is surrounded by seas in all directions except for a land connection in the southeast to the desolate Egasach desert, and to the east connected to the Ekyunimoy mountains. Of note are the Sarmadfar Sea alongside the Elarbarc gulf, the Glorelthir coastline, the Randrunnse gulf, and the Broken Sea to the far north studded with icebergs.

The Domandrod is a vast forest wilderness teeming with wildlife, Fey, and aberrations. Within its borders the Feyrealm pokes into the Material Plane, causing fantastical and terrifying landscapes. Surrounding the Domandrod, Eordand is primarily composed of forests and grasslands pierced with rivers, many of which originate from the Domandrod. These surrounding lands may not be half within the Feyrealm, but its magical influence is still strong, moreso than the effect of the Day of Ashen Skies. 

In the western coast along the Glorelthir and Milsgelp coastline the Spring Court grows in eternal bloom, situated where the season of Spring is the strongest. Along the southern coastline of the Sarmadfar the Summer Court basks in the warmth of the sun and the cool ocean waves, where the Summer is the strongest. In the far north the Winter Court ekes a living in the half-frozen taiga, where Winter strikes every year the strongest. North of the Spring Court, the Autumn Court's homeland along the Fogarabh wages eternal war and preparation against the Winter. 

Eordand is dominated by the grand cities of Arakeprun and Murdkather, both being built on the bones of Precursor Ruins. Across Eordand Precursor ruins dot the landscape, some piercing the heavens while others are buried beneath the earth. From these ancient structures countless treasures can be found, some mundane while others containing vast knowledge and power.

J84.title: The Warlords of Taychend
J84.lore:
Though Taychend was spared complete destruction from the Day of Ashen Skies, the worst excesses of Elvenkind – pride, ambition, envy – doomed it nonetheless. As society collapsed, newly-freed slaves and tyrant-slavers alike divided Taychend into innumerable warlord states, centred around great cities built on the salvaged ruins of the precursors.

Rural mansions were converted into scrap-fortresses, libraries and temples turned to new hubs of trade and vice, and the magical trinkets and artefacts of the precursor Slaver-Nobles repurposed into weapons of mass destruction, wielded by powerful “Relic-Lords” exemplified by the infamous Ebenanth Starshear, who conquered the land a mere four decades after the ruin. This proved a second apocalypse, as his magic blasted cities apart and annihilated armies in an instant.

Since Ebenanth’s assassination, and the subsequent collapse of his empire, Taychend has been consumed by the Cycle of Warlordism, a socio-political system dominated by endemic warfare, strong rulers, and the rise and fall of kingdoms and empires. Armies tens of thousands strong march to the beat of their warlord leaders, while faith itself fuels the fire, as tens of millions of elves struggle to achieve great things in the quest for godhood. Innumerable cults worship the warriors, conquerors, and master-artisans of ages past.

Taychend has been united six times. Twice by the Relic-Lords, once by the Mudaliars of Nanru Nakar, once by the Chendhyans and twice by the Gophirans. No Empire has stood more than 150 years before collapsing entirely – most did not last even a quarter as long – but it is only a matter of time. Taychend will unite again.

Recent History

In 1316, the Second Gophiran Empire finally collapsed. After over a century of dominance, a series of cataclysmic civil wars wracked the empire, dividing their prestigious Ironblood army and once more destroying any semblance of higher order in Taychend. For the past hundred years, Taychend has been gripped by regional fighting and chaos.

In the steppe-savannah known as the Guaraddhi, Chendhyans and Taychendi struggle for control. Zaernsarlan the Ravager, at the head of the greatest Chendhyan Ezhudar since the days of the old Saerraeg Empire, has dominated the lower Guaraddhi for forty years, razing the old Slaver-City of Kannagarendi and pushing back the Bloodcloaks of Rakkabuttai. But Zaernsarlan grows old – it is only a matter of time before he ascends to the heavens, and then it is anyone’s guess who could replace him.

In the Kalavend, the kingdom of Nanru Nakar, traditionally the most powerful of the Kalavendhi kingdoms and home to the largest city in Aelantir by far, has been riven by a succession crisis: the War of Three Brothers, caused by the death of their last unified king at the hands of Erankar nearly 10 years ago. Meanwhile, the wealthy cities of the lower Kalavend prepare for war – Larankar marches again, and if Nanru Nakar is too divided to stop them, the other Kalavendhi must rise to the occasion.

In the 1420s, Enlarmai emerged victorious after almost a hundred years of war against their eternal rival, Urvand, slaughtering the Urvandi royal family – bar a single nine year-old child, who escaped. Ten years later, Erankar the Defiant reclaimed his city and put Enlarmai to the torch, razing the city to its foundations. Though Erankar’s ambitions were delayed outside the walls of Nanru Nakar, they were not sundered, and the Eighth Kingdom of Larankar now prepares to launch a war of conquest not seen in hundreds of years – with all Taychend as the prize.

Ameion. The newest foreign invader to threaten the Taychendi way of life, feared ever since he seized Anakalnakori and then defeated a coalition of Taychendi states at the First Battle of Bukénthia. In so doing he slayed the King of Gophira in single combat and shattered his remnant Kingdom for good. All of Yodhanchend and Thektoram braces itself for Laskaris the Conqueror’s next campaign – but whether they can stand up to the advance of Ameion’s dreaded phalanx is anyone’s guess.

F58.title: A Fleeting Age of Glory
F58.lore:
Hush child, dry your tears and run as far as you can, flee to the coast and follow the Melgarkas, this new age will not be kind to us, but we will endure, as we have endured all others. The Storm will come again, our Mother has not forgotten us.

Akasik is a land of storms and silver. Located in the northwesternmost corner of Sarhal, it stands at the crossroads between Kheterata, Cannor and Fangaula, more often than not the contact point between the latter two.

Ancient history

The Akasi trace the origins of their civilization to the Age of the Storm, a time when the god-like Storm Queens ruled the land. Under their rule the Akasi prospered and theirs became one of the greatest kingdoms in the Divenhal. The invasion of Kheterata and the death of the Storm Queens put an end to this age.

After the Age of the Storm came the Age of the River, when the Deep Devils arose from the sea and slaughtered all they faced; and after that the Age of the Moon, when the Day of Ashen Skies tore the land asunder. The Akasi wouldn’t see another great age until the arrival of Sedoric I, who united the 3 kingdoms of Akasik and led them to a new age of glory.

Recent history

All ages must pass as the aspects do, however, and this newfound glory came to an end after the gnollish invasion of Viakkoc. The three kingdoms, mighty as they were, found themselves divided and weak against the coordinated gnollish pack fleeing from their defeat at the hands of Gnollakaz. The scourge broke through the defences of Khasa and slaughtered the stonewing harpies that lived there, using their blood as fuel for their demonflame and rituals, enslaving the rest of the once proud kingdom of Khasa. The Deshaki have knelt before a Tefori king seeking protection, and the merchants of Ekha committed the greatest of betrayals, opening the gates of the city and granting tribute to the gnolls, sending their own to die so they may live one day more.

The great ages of glory are behind us now, but not all is lost - a new storm will come, and Akasik will be free once more.

F39.lore:
Bulwar is an arid land to the east of the Divenhal inhabited primarily by the native Bulwari humans, in addition to Sun Elves, harpies in the northern hills, Exodus Goblins in Bahar, and gnolls to its southern border with the Salahad.

At the center of Bulwar lies the fertile Suran River Valley, a large watershed that feeds the region and exits at the eastern portion of the Divenhal Sea, whereas to its north it is flanked by the Harpy Hills, a mountainous region inhabited by its namesake, and the Serpentreach Mountains from whence came the goblins. In the east, the Jadd spreads across the deserts from the Mountain of Clear Sight, seen as heretics by the other Sun Elves and humans in Bulwar. Between Bulwar proper and the Far Salahad, the gnolls have pushed their way to the Upper Suran and consolidated in the Šad Sur mountains.

Historically the Bulwari peoples have been divided into vying city-states who have had a shared history of oppression, initially by the Genies, then the gnolls and harpies and eventually other human nations such as Castanor, the Damerian Republic and Kheterata. Their liberation by the Sun Elves and Jaher in 1001AA marks a new period of prosperity for those willing to accept them as Surael’s Chosen people. Since then, the Sun Elves have held Bulwar under their dominion, and in 1444AA, human nations within Bulwar are vassals to the Sun Elven states.

H27.lore:
The Ynn River is the lifeblood of Ynnic Ruinborn Elves and their civilization. In the aftermath of the Day of Ashen Skies, the Ynnic plateau was carved out and its cities annihilated. When the first Ynnics arrived to the Ynn, they found a near-absence of Precursor Relics and ruins, and in time the Precursor Elves were forgotten, giving way to other legends such as Lukaus and the Antler Lords, The Ending Flood, and the Kingdoms of Malacnar and Vareynn.

In 1444, two centuries have passed since the collapse of the Ynnic Empire, albeit its capital Arverynn still clings on with a claimant to the imperial dynasty. Sarda is divided into small realms, though the rising star of Trompolere is set on a collision course against this status quo.

In Dolindha, the resurgent Kingdom of Malacnar sharpens its blades while the lordship of Amacimst, first of the feudal old guard, rides to meet the storm head-on. To the west, the Drozma Tur cultists still lick their wounds after their failed invasion, but the Duke of Pomvasonn will soon make his move. Surely no outside power will come to interfere?

The Ynn River and its many tributaries offer many stretches of fertile land, although in 1444 only the main stem of the river is controlled by the sedentary Ynnic Ruinborn Elves. In time the outskirt regions of Veykoda to the east and the Epednan Expanse to the west will be colonized by Cannorians looking for a new life in Aelantir: primarily humans, but also elves, dwarves, halflings, and half-orcs.

The Ynn River is also the region's main physical obstacle: its strong currents, only partially contained by the Great Ynnic Dams, and the monsters lurking below make the crossing of armies a foolhardy endeavor. Only the Great Ynnic Dams provide safe crossing, acting as bridges for armies and priceless strategic points, in addition to their economic and religious value.

Of paramount importantance are the dams of Vels Bacar and Mocbarja, guarding the only route of expansion from the northern regions of Rzenta and Dolindha into the southern lands of the Sarda and viceversa, until new avenues for conquest will be opened up by the creation of Cannorian settlements. The dams of Adbrabohvi and Bosancovac, meanwhile, are situated on the outskirts of Ynnic Civilization and will become important contacts points with the Cannorian world.

To the south, the Ynn River Valley ends abruptly at the great Cliffs of Ruin, which many Ynnic Ruinborn Elves believe to be the end of the world, and can be descended only by building Cliff Passages at a few key points.

To the west and east, the Ynn River Valley is limited by the Ekyunimoy Mountains and the Dalairey Range, from which spring the many tributaries of the Ynn River.

Going north, the Ynn River Valley is occupied by the fey forests of Rzenta and the unassailable Forest of the Cursed Ones, which open up into the arctic shores of the Broken Sea where Istranar or Cannorian colonists may have made their landing.

A gap in the south-west leads to the arid land of Haraf, which the Epednar used centuries ago to migrate to the Ynn's western outskirts. Venturing north-west there is a passage into the Precursor Relics-rich region of Eordand skirting the Ekyunimoy Mountains and the Randrunnse Gulf.

Y17.title: Land of the Stone Army
Y17.lore:
On the ledge stood a motley group of fighters, looking over the scorched and blood soaked fields that only hours before were filled with thousands of men. They stood battered, soaked in rain, in blood and mud, tired yet above all victorious. For the second time in a season they had beaten the invading armies of Zhiqian and Beikdugang, who in their arrogance looked upon the lands of Gushuokguan and saw easy conquest. They sought to divide this land of the beaten, exiled and downtrodden for their own gain, but this had proven a fool's dream.

“Now it is time to collect the dead and get them back to their families”, called Captain Lu. Lu helped as well, as in the Stone Army, an officer is higher only to coordinate, in all else he is an equal. He needed to find the body of his brother Bao. Lu would cover him in the cloth he had been given by his mother to keep him warm during the cold nights. He had promised her that Bao would return as soon as possible, for he was needed during the harvest.

Lu’s story was a common one. Brothers had lost brothers, fathers sons and sons fathers. All had fought side by side to defend the small patch of land they called home. For this was a land unlike others in Haless: it was free from the oppression of noble lords, the scheming of corrupt eunuchs and the greed of the city merchants. It was a land in which all were allowed to buy or lend a patch of land to turn into their own little slice of paradise, no matter their background, race or creed. Every soul was free in Gushuokguan, as long as they respected their neighbours and stood ready for battle when called upon.

A16.title: Wineport, Leader of the League of Winebay
A16.lore:
’...Under this League are the cities of Wineport, Foxalley, Coruan and Bottlepoint and the lands surrounding them bound together, to seek their independence and freedom, and to bring wealth to their citizens with the trade of good and wholesome wines.’
-Extract from the Covenant of the League of Winebay, 1419

Primer
Wineport, officially known as the ‘League of Winebay’, is a relatively new state in the lands of Lencenor. It arose out of the collapse of the now defunct Duchy of Íselsuin after the duke perished in 1419 during the Third Lilac War (1407-1421) and left no heir, leading to the cities of the Winebay seeking their own path during the chaotic times that this created. This has left them as influential traders in the Bay of Wines, though one with many powerful enemies, such as the Duchy of Rubenaire, who captured Coruan during the chaos of Íselsuin’s collapse.

Recent History
Wineport remained neutral in the Fourth Lilac War (1422-1443), Though it consumed practically every nation surrounding them - they chose to be after the Moon Party sold suzerainty of Toarnaire to the League. They used the time to become highly influential as traders in the League of Winebay, and to supply mercenaries to both sides. This has granted the cities of the League great wealth, becoming some of the biggest cities in the Bay of Wines. Now that the Lilac Wars have reached their conclusion, the current Lord Mayor of Wineport, and the head of the League, Énri the Taster seeks two things: first, to maintain the League’s independence in the face of a resurgent Lorent and the Wine Lords of Rubenaire and Eilísin, who look at our lands hungrily after the cost of loss in the War. And, after that is secured, to fulfil the goal of the original League’s document - to become the greatest producers and traders of wine in all of Lencenor.

G26.title: The Lordship of Amacimst
G26.lore:
Protectors of Dolindha, let thine names be known, thy aims honourable and thy sword swift and true.

Primer
Amacimst, Castle of the East. This is the name that protected Dolindha ever since the fall of the Ynnic Empire in 1205. Alongside it stood Amaceped, Castle of the West. The lords of the two castles ruled in peace and magnanimity until the Great Heathen Army of the Rzentur attacked.

The battle of Lisicalrevo saw the heathens repelled by the valour of Ynnic Knights, but Amaceped fell soon, treacherously assaulted by the brutes of Malacnar. Alone, Amacimst began its decline, losing its allies and vassals in a series of betrayals.

Amacimst's current Lord, Gelendirn Sivasred, is known for his idleness in all affairs of state that do not involve writing long letters or attending plays, but out there in the fields Gelendirn's son already rallies the knights, promising he will wage war for as long as enemies remain on the Ynn.

G35.title: Arverynn, Teal Crest of the Ynnic Empire
G35.lore:
"Take me back to the Northward River, as I sing a song; a floating rhyme rippling through time towards the City of Forever."
-To the Garrisons' opening verse, Talvyr yen Arkilodo, Sarda Poet.

Primer
Arverynn, Jewel of the Ynn, City of Emperors, Mound-Fortress: these and many more titles echo across the art and memories of Ynnic Ruinborn. Founded in 900 after Flood by the Soldier-Emperor Calrodiy the Great, first of his name, Arverynn shone as a brand-new beacon of civilization at a time when the majority of elvenkind was living at sea or surrounded by ruins.

As beautiful as it was impregnable, only the perverted actions of the dragon Drozmagog and his cultists could hope to strike at the imperial capital, and even that didn't last - the damage inflicted by the Great Fire of Arverynn in 1028 was quickly undone through a mobilization of the Ynnic Empire.

The Third Ynnic Civil War saw the dissolution of the Ynnic Empire and the ruin of its urban centers; despite this, Arverynn's ruin did not come from an outside conqueror, but from within, when in 1205 after Flood a famished mob lynched Emperor Fineas Vyrekynn. A most foul event, which spelled the end of Calrodiy's line and of the Ynnic Empire. Or so people thought…

Recent History
Arverynnics have a name for the 13th and 14th centuries: the Age of Petty Lords. A string of minor nobles ruled the great city, trying to manage its shrinking populace while fighting off economic decline, the odd rabble-rousing preacher and the schemes of their own court. Some of these rulers appealed to Arverynn's prestige, but they struggled subduing Stanyrhrada or keeping the Veykodan raiders at bay. Reuniting Sarda, let alone the whole Empire, seemed a pipe dream. The Lordship of Arverynn was destined to become a backwater, eclipsed by the feudal Dolindhan powers of Amaceped and Amacimst and by the nearby Vels Bacar…

Then in 1386, the Emperor returned. Calrodiy IV announced himself as the last scion of the Vyrekynn dynasty, as he emerged from the horizon at the head of a company of shining knights and mercenaries. The petty lord who was occupying Arverynn did not dare resist him, disappearing into the night as his name was forgotten by history. The city celebrated Calrodiy as a liberator, and he returned this warmth with weeks of festivities. However, this victory would be Calrodiy's first and last.

The aspiring emperor overestimated his neighbours' willingness to submit to him and rejoin the glorious empire, losing the chance for peaceful reunification with his diplomatic blunders. His lavish festivities put a dent in the coffers, and he alienated even his closest supporters, first through unrealistic and insistent demands, like when he asked the captain of his knights to "tell his soldiers to bring better armour for tomorrow's battle", then by becoming cruel as his frustrations grew. In 1444, Calrodiy IV has one foot in the grave, his defeat against the young Alaran of Trompolere still bleeding him of his pride.

Despite these circumstances, it is not our lot to despair. The lives of Ynnic elves are short, and their wisdom fallible. But there is no doubt in the mind of the citizens that we are primed for greatness, that no matter how deep we sink or how violently the earth trembles, we will emerge and thrive. Arverynn is 500 years old: an impressive age, if this were a Veykodan hamlet. For the capital of the Ynn ten thousand years would not suffice, so let us move on, for there is much to live for and much to be done, as long as the Ynn wills it.

G27.title: The Republic of Brelar
G27.lore:
Primer

North of the grand castles of Dolindha lies a dreary range of mountains, grey is their stone and barren-seeming their surfaces. Here the Brelynn flows north, in stark contrast to other Ynnic rivers, and here since immemorial has lain the town of Brelar, or Siva Brel, "The Grey Hill". 

Tracing its origins further back than any other ynnic civilisation save perhaps the Battlekings of Malacnar, the nation of Brelar has ever been a republic. In ancient days the leaders of Old Brefineis were chosen among the wise and elderly to sit in council and lead their people toward a safe future. Though the republic fell under the rule of Vareynn it never fully lost its traditions, and the fierce and enduring soldiers of the grey hills proved invaluable to the rise of the Dolindhan Kingdom and the formation of the Ynnic Empire. 

In a surprising twist of history, the line of the first Emperor was abruptly ended in 761 leaving the Ynnics leaderless and in turmoil. With no legitimate heir of the yen Dolin dynasty to be found the Empire soon devolved into squabbles of rulership, until an unlikely candidate was crowned Vitsar: Demirad I yen Brelar, a Patrician’s son loosely related to the Dolin family via an old political marriage from the days when Brelar and Vareynn were close. The yen Brelar proved a capable dynasty of ruthless administrative ability and zero tolerance for corruption, during the reign of the sons of Demirad from 761 to 840, the Empire’s provinces were redrawn, its roads and harbours well travelled and prosperous, and its functionaries honest and efficient as never seen before or since. Though due to a lack of military campaigning the Brelari dynasty falls somewhat below the Dolin and Vyrekynn in prominence, having prioritised the flow of life along the river rather than achieving any grand victories. 

When the Ynnic Empire fell an effort was made by Brelar to gather what people and knowledge they could rescue to the safety of the Grey Hill, hoping to one day bring a return of their knowledge and expertise to the whole of the Great River. Yet after centuries passed with no end to the post-collapse warfare and warlording of petty ynnic lords even the memory of Brelar is fading. It may not be long before the secrets of the Ynnic Empire and perhaps ones even older are extinguished forever, whether by the negligence of time or by the sword of some upstart Lord.

Recent History

In 1444 the Republic of Brelar lies threatened on all sides, hatred of republicanism has grown within ynnic culture since the rise of feudalism and strongest is this hate in Amacimst, who boast the greatest knights of the Ynn and greedily eye the forges and masonries of their northern neighbour. In central Dolindha a new upstart has claimed the title of Battleking of Malacnar, and as ancient rivals to the ancestors of Brelar no doubt has ambitions to march his army of heroes to tear asunder the marble gates. Far to the west the armies of Rzenta are regrouping, though their hate of republicanism is not so great as the Dolindhans’ it is unlikely that the Republic would survive the approach of a second Heathen Host. 

Hope of course is not lost in Brelar, for the republic has stood a thousand years by the actions and knowledge of its gruff and enduring people and by the Grace of the Great River it shall endure yet a millennia more.

Y20.title: Feiten, into Azure Skies and Emerald Seas
Y20.lore:
The fishermen of Feiten set out into the ocean as the sun rises, just as their fathers did, and just as their fathers' fathers did. Drifting through seemingly endless beds of glowing jellyfish, they have little reason to believe that their children will experience anything different. As the shore slowly recedes from view, paper lanterns released for the prior night's Jellyfish Festival strike a prominent silhouette against the lightening heavens. And in that brief moment, the thoughts of the fishermen are united as they look upward toward the mirror of the sky and wonder what it'd be like to float amongst the lanterns.

Primer
Jaher and his Phoenix Legion's ill-fated conquest of Yanshen was - as war often is - brutal, disruptive, and destructive. As Jaher's Legions advanced along the Yanhe, many refugees dispersed south into the sparsely populated Jellyfish Coast. There, they hoped that the Coast's rugged hills and hidden valleys would shelter them from the inevitable rising of Jaher's sun. It was these refugees and their descendants, coalescing after the chaos that was the ousting of the Phoenix, that formed the League of Feiten.

Recent History
Though a relatively new entity in Yanshen, Feiten has nevertheless managed to grow rich and catapult itself into relevance off the back of mercantile power. Efforts to convert this wealth into regional influence has been mixed, however. A recent war with the southern savages of Zyujyut has netted Feiten the gateway to the tea jungle, Cuihiok, but this conquest came at the steep cost of most of Feiten's fleet, stolen away by the pirate queen Ping Shuang of Pinghoi. To the west, the efforts of Feiten and Tianlou has led to the splintering of Xiyun. Even now, those two countries fight to imprint their influence on the fledgling Luoyip. This conflict is only exacerbated by the fact that Tianlou, the jewel of Yanshen, has far and away the richest and most well-established merchant fleets this side of the Kharunyana. If Feiten wishes to leave its mark on the world, conflict with the city of temples and their entrenched eunuchs and traders is inevitable.

The city of Feiten itself lurches toward a flickering future. For centuries, the harbour had been the launching point for minor whaling expeditions by the natives of the area. Everyone knew that whale oil burned the longest and cleanest of any animal fat, and the small amount harvested with every whale hunt was perfect to use in the annual celebratory Jellyfish Festival to fill the skies with floating lanterns. But, why waste something with such interesting properties on mere toys? With Feiten looking for any advantage against its rivals, the true potential of whale oil has only just started to be realized...

G32.title: The Kingdom of Malacnar
G32.lore:
"If you ask an elf in Adbrabohvi what caused the Ending Flood, you'll be told it was because people didn't pray enough. Ask an elf in Vareynn, it was because people didn't fight hard enough. Ask an elf in Malacnar, and you'll be told that the Ending Flood was the best thing that ever happened to this land, sweeping away the old order to deliver them a world of fighting, their Paradise of the Blade."
-Lord Talvynn of Selocshana

Primer
Malacnar! City of Warriors, Home of the Blade, House of Battle, for centuries at war and for centuries in rebellion, exporter of teachers in the martial arts and undyingly loyal mercenaries. Founded by the mythical Warrior-King Levodas, Malacnar is if not the Ynn River's oldest state post Day of Ashen Skies, the oldest one whose name isn't lost to history.

And one has to wonder how such fierce history could deliver Malacnar intact to the present day. After eclipsing its old rival Stantirvare, Malacnar was in turn conquered: in 304 AA at the Battle of Drevonbost King Komerjon of Vareynn bested Malacnar's Battleking Vatrevid IV in battle and slew all his warriors; and still Malacnar became a thorn in its conqueror's side, cyclically rebelling in the Hundred Years' Uprising.

The founding of the Ynnic Empire in 625 resulted in a civil war, and the City of Warriors backed the right Antler Horse - for helping Empress Zeldja against the oligarchs of Vareynn's Rider Assembly, Malacnar was rewarded with lenient taxes, the lifting of the survellaince previously imposed on the city, and the creation of the Malacnar Battleguards, an elite unit which kept Malacnar's name high across the Ynn.

Recent History
Under the Ynnic Empire Malacnar's wrath subsided and its people assimilated into broader Dolindhan culture. After the Empire disintegrated Malacnar remained a quiet subject to a Dolindhan lord - still, the idea of a Kingdom of warriors led by a fearless Battleking remained a vivid image in the imagination of Ynnic Ruinborn Elves.

This image sparked Galadorn, older brother of the Lord of Amaceped, to launch in 1377 a rebellion: he unearthed the Sword of Varmeljon, symbol of the Hundred Years' Uprising carefully hidden away in a family crypt, and proclaimed that the Kingdom of Malacnar would be reborn.

Malacnar then defeated the forces sent to pacify it, and went on the offensive storming the castle of Amaceped. With much of Dolindha spent after the recent Rzentur invasion, the coalition had to relent and recognize its independence. The Lord of Stantirshalas, fallen into a Malacnari ambush, swore fealty to them.

This reversal thrusted Malacnar into power: the surrounding lords now look at Malacnar with wary eyes, eager to restore the feudal order, and Malacnar warriors are ready to welcome them spear in hand. And where weapons can't get through, the new Battleking Alvarad Zabrmon - equal part warrior and speaker - will ensure Malacnar won't repeat old mistakes.

B17.title: The Dwarven Adventurers: Asra Expedition
B17.lore:
"Be it resolved that: 1) An expeditionary force shall be assembled, with composition as outlined in Appendix 2 ... iii) Led by Jarmun Asra ... 2) Reclamation profits shall be used as seed funding for further Serpentspine endeavours ... loan repayment shall follow Schedule IV ... Endnote: Our fiduciary duty mandates that profits must take precedence over prestige or any idea of 'Dwarven glory'."
-Fragments of the Asra Bank's charter, establishing funding for an expedition to the Serpentspine, 1443

Primer
Once, the dwarven people stretched across the entirety of the Serpentspine Mountain Range, from its westernmost borders at Escann to the farthest reaches of Yanshen. Once, all dwarf-kind was unified under the Empire of Aul-Dwarov, All-Dwarves-Home, and had experienced their golden age long before humanity built their first castles.

But that was millennia ago. Aul-Dwarov slowly crumbled, as many Holds fell to the Orcs and Goblins. The central government, the Amlharaz, collapsed with the fall of the old capital of the empire, Amldihr. The great Dwarven Rail maintained by Er-Natvir, which once carried ale from Hul-az-Krakazol and scientific discoveries from Dûr-Vazhatun across the entire Serpentspine, fell into disrepair.

The long, grim, Last Days of the Dwarovar ended when countless numbers of Orcs poured out of Hul-Jorkad. This ferocious rush across the Serpentspine shattered Holds, slaughtered dwarves, and invited even the most cowardly goblin to infest the tunnels of the mountains, leaving much of the Dwarovar in ruins.

However not every region was as devastated by the invading forces as the west had been. The Segbandal was revived in the Middle Dwarovar and thrived for millennia as the strong alliance managed to begin reclamation attempts in the west, prosper in a golden age and greatly aid the people of Bulwar. In the East Serpentspine, the Jade Empire was proclaimed by the hold of Grônstunad, claiming the title of High King and rightful heirs to the lost empire. They consolidated all the holds in their region while repelling goblins, ogres and hobgoblins from taking advantage of the chaos caused by the orcs in the west. While making enemies with the Segbandal, they managed to greatly influence the humans of Rahen and Vimdatrong and constructed the hold of Verkal Ozovar. In the end however, both the Segbandal and Jade Empire had failed in containing the menaces that beset them, leading to their falls in more recent history.

Recent History
The last Dwarven Hold to fall in the west was Khugdihr, broken by Gurukk Siegebreaker in 1424. But this was only a harbinger to the Greentide, when Korgus Dookanson unified the clans and led them pouring out of the Serpentspine into Escann and later the Deepwoods.

But the flood of orcs and goblins out of the mountains and Korgus' defeat by Corin in 1444 meant opportunity: the long-abandoned mountains were now emptier than before, and ripe for reclamation. Dwarves from the exodus states of Rubyhold and Silverforge who live amongst humanity, and the rich Asra Bank have poured funding into multiple expeditions. You are one of the many adventurers, sons and daughters of your forefathers who fled to the surface long ago, who now seek to return to the lands of your ancestors.

The road ahead will not be easy. The caverns and abandoned roads of the Dwarovrod are treacherous, and any maps have long since rotted away. Hundreds of thousands of goblins and orcs still remain, both wandering the mountains and organised into squatter states and rampaging warbands of their own. The challenges will be fierce, but ahead of you lays the ultimate prize: refounding an ancient Hold, digging deeper than ever before, and re-establishing Dwarven rule over the Serpentspine. Perhaps one day, not too long from now, Aul-Dwarov shall rise anew, even greater than before.

It only became darker when the 52nd Castan was made a puppet by the Sorcerer-King Nichmer, who then led Black Castanor in brutal wars until most of Cannor fell under his dominion. The knights of Adenica were slaughtered with their proud steeds, the Blademarches' legendary sword, Calindal, the Gleaming Blade was overcome, and the Marrodic were forced into their mountains.

B82.title: The Resurgent Forest Goblins: Petalmasher
B82.lore:
"Forest Goblin, Outsider Goblin. What’s the difference? Everyone out there wants you enslaved or dead. Do you think they care whether our eyes glow yellow or not?" - Cleesb Spiked Log

Recent History
Goblins have lived in the Deepwoods for much, much longer than orcs or elves. Their civilization had long passed its zenith by the time the Wood Elves arrived, an event that broke what little control they had left over the Deepwoods. Consigned to the dark corners of the groves and glades, the Forest Goblin tribes endured, consigned to obscurity.

Then, three things happened that drastically and irrevocably changed the Deepwoods. First, the goblin warlord Oubblig won a string of wars that saw much of the southwest Deepwoods fall under goblin rule. Then, an immense invasion of orcs tore into the Deepwoods from the north, defeating many of the elven clans and bringing many goblin slaves along with them. Finally, the Verdant Veil fell, once again opening travel out of the Deepwoods. With the elves and orcs at each other’s throats, many of the enslaved goblins escaped and joined our ranks, and with the outside world now open to us, there has never been an opportunity like this for the goblins of the Deepwoods.

Still there is division. Oubblig is dead, his diminished realm split between Oubbligschild and Brambleskinner. Verminhusk is utterly surrounded by orcish tribes, while the Spiked Log clan’s acceptance of many runaway slaves has led Relgakk Blackmoon to vow to hunt down every last goblin in the Deepwoods. Goblins are still outnumbered by orcs and elves alike, and the old rivalry between the Pactseeker goblins and those that follow the Shaman Old Ways has far from disappeared. But even with these desperate conditions, this may be the day when the goblins rise to greatness.

They were joined by a plethora of adventuring companies, from as far afield as Lorent and Kheterata, as well as the Count's League, a remnant band of Castellyrian counts led by Carleon Blacktower, and a force of griffon-riders led by Prince Gryf 'the Young'.

This force dealt the Greentide some of its first defeats, waging a bloody stalemate at the Battle of Rottenstep. Among the many that fell there was Corin, a red-headed squire of Bennon — but that night, she arose as an avatar of the fallen god Agrados. Reborn and renewed, she founded the adventuring party Corin's Circle, rallied the Marcher Lords, and led them all the way to Castonath itself. There, while defending Lothane 'Bluetusk' Silmuna with her shield, and killing Dookanson with her sword, she was slain for the second time. And this time, she would not rise.

That was yesterday, the 10th of Nerament. Now, the morning after, the sun rises upon a new Escann, even if it is still infested by orcish and goblin warbands. Will you reforge one of the kingdoms of old? Or create something new? Perhaps one day all Cannor will again be reshaped by the will of an Escanni Emperor.

H68.title: The Dwarven Adventurers: Ruby Company
H68.lore:
"Be it resolved that: 1) An expeditionary force shall be assembled, with composition as outlined in Appendix 2 ... iii) Led by Jarmun Asra ... 2) Reclamation profits shall be used as seed funding for further Serpentspine endeavours ... loan repayment shall follow Schedule IV ... Endnote: Our fiduciary duty mandates that profits must take precedence over prestige or any idea of 'Dwarven glory'."
-Fragments of the Asra Bank's charter, establishing funding for an expedition to the Serpentspine, 1443

Primer
Once, the dwarven people stretched across the entirety of the Serpentspine Mountain Range, from its westernmost borders at Escann to the farthest reaches of Yanshen. Once, all dwarf-kind was unified under the Empire of Aul-Dwarov, All-Dwarves-Home, and had experienced their golden age long before humanity built their first castles.

But that was millennia ago. Aul-Dwarov slowly crumbled, as many Holds fell to the Orcs and Goblins. The central government, the Amlharaz, collapsed with the fall of the old capital of the empire, Amldihr. The great Dwarven Rail maintained by Er-Natvir, which once carried ale from Hul-az-Krakazol and scientific discoveries from Dûr-Vazhatun across the entire Serpentspine, fell into disrepair.

The long, grim, Last Days of the Dwarovar ended when countless numbers of Orcs poured out of Hul-Jorkad. This ferocious rush across the Serpentspine shattered Holds, slaughtered dwarves, and invited even the most cowardly goblin to infest the tunnels of the mountains, leaving much of the Dwarovar in ruins.

However not every region was as devastated by the invading forces as the west had been. The Segbandal was revived in the Middle Dwarovar and thrived for millennia as the strong alliance managed to begin reclamation attempts in the west, prosper in a golden age and greatly aid the people of Bulwar. In the East Serpentspine, the Jade Empire was proclaimed by the hold of Grônstunad, claiming the title of High King and rightful heirs to the lost empire. They consolidated all the holds in their region while repelling goblins, ogres and hobgoblins from taking advantage of the chaos caused by the orcs in the west. While making enemies with the Segbandal, they managed to greatly influence the humans of Rahen and Vimdatrong and constructed the hold of Verkal Ozovar. In the end however, both the Segbandal and Jade Empire had failed in containing the menaces that beset them, leading to their falls in more recent history.

Recent History
The last Dwarven Hold to fall in the west was Khugdihr, broken by Gurukk Siegebreaker in 1424. But this was only a harbinger to the Greentide, when Korgus Dookanson unified the clans and led them pouring out of the Serpentspine into Escann and later the Deepwoods.

But the flood of orcs and goblins out of the mountains and Korgus' defeat by Corin in 1444 meant opportunity: the long-abandoned mountains were now emptier than before, and ripe for reclamation. Dwarves from the exodus states of Rubyhold and Silverforge who live amongst humanity, and the rich Asra Bank have poured funding into multiple expeditions. You are one of the many adventurers, sons and daughters of your forefathers who fled to the surface long ago, who now seek to return to the lands of your ancestors.

The road ahead will not be easy. The caverns and abandoned roads of the Dwarovrod are treacherous, and any maps have long since rotted away. Hundreds of thousands of goblins and orcs still remain, both wandering the mountains and organised into squatter states and rampaging warbands of their own. The challenges will be fierce, but ahead of you lays the ultimate prize: refounding an ancient Hold, digging deeper than ever before, and re-establishing Dwarven rule over the Serpentspine. Perhaps one day, not too long from now, Aul-Dwarov shall rise anew, even greater than before.

You are one of the many adventurers, sons and daughters of your forefathers who fled to the surface long ago, who now seek to return to the lands of your ancestors.

The road ahead will not be easy. The caverns and abandoned roads of the Dwarovrod are treacherous, and any maps have long since rotted away. Hundreds of thousands of goblins and orcs still remain, both wandering the mountains and organised into squatter states and rampaging warbands of their own. The challenges will be fierce, but ahead of you lays the ultimate prize: refounding an ancient Hold, digging deeper than ever before, and re-establishing Dwarven rule over the Serpentspine. Perhaps one day, not too long from now, Aul-Dwarov shall rise anew, even greater than before.

H74.lore:
"Always keep three daggers hidden on you. One for your enemy, one for your friend, and another to stab yourself and frame your boss."
-Rule 222 in "Da Ultimate Rulez for Survival" by Muk the Bloodless, died of blood loss 1571.

Primer
Born as one of, if not the weakest race, goblins rely on pragmatism, cunning, and extreme cruelty (even when dealing with kin) to gain every advantage, a survival instinct that has permeated deep into goblin society where lives are just a relatively cheap commodity. They are known to live in innumerable numbers inside the Serpentspine's dark depths, and claim to have existed in the mountains long before the dwarven empire Aul-Dwarov dominated the region. Despite that empire's advanced technology, they remained a constant thorn and irritant, raiding unprotected Er-Natvir trains and ambushing any dwarves who strayed too far outside the walls of a Hold. And, of course, they would outlast that decrepit empire, watching with glee during the millennia-long Last Days of the Dwarovar.

When the orcs emerged however, rushing out of Hul-Jorkad, the goblins faced a new threat. They enslaved countless clans, and many were slaughtered even as they achieved the long-held dream of seeing the dwarven holds smashed before them (some, like Verkal Kozenad, fell fast; others, like Gor Ozumbrog, only fell to a goblin horde when betrayed by its allies).

That history meant many goblins knew what was coming when the Dookanson rose and united the orcs behind him. Some fled, retreating to the darkest and most inhospitable caves. Others escaped the Serpentspine, flooding into Bulwar and Bahar as the Exodus Goblins and leading to the Decades of Devastation. The most unfortunate, however, could not escape the Dookanson's slavery and became nothing but cannon fodder and servants during the Greentide.

Recent History
In the end, however, goblins outlasted the Dookanson, just as they outlasted Aul-Dwarov. Now, for the first time in many centuries, goblins are free from the oppression of any empire. Among others, Railskulker has claimed the ancient Railyards, Snotfinger plans revenge on the despised orcs, and Greedy Grin lusts for the gold of Verkal Gulan.

But the fate of the goblin race remains uncertain. Greedy dwarven adventurers from the outside world invade the Serpentspine once again, while decrepit Remnant Holds are bright with light, seemingly rousing from their long sleep. Meanwhile, word from the east says that goblins have been subjugated there by a hobgoblin Command.

No more! The goblins will reclaim their homes once and for all. They will rise from the obscure darkness and unite the tribes into an empire that will rule the whole of Serpentspine. By conquest, plunder, flames, explosions, and whatever other schemes can be dreamed up, it is now up to you to claim the mountains and see goblins rise as they never have before.

In the end, however, goblins outlasted the Dookanson, just as they outlasted Aul-Dwarov. Now, for the first time in many centuries, goblins are free from the oppression of any empire. Among others, Railskulker has claimed the ancient Railyards, Snotfinger plans revenge on the despised orcs, and Greedy Grin lusts for the gold of Verkal Gulan.

But the fate of the goblin race remains uncertain. Greedy dwarven adventurers from the outside world invade the Serpentspine once again, while decrepit Remnant Holds are bright with light, seemingly rousing from their long sleep. Meanwhile, word from the east says that goblins have been subjugated there by a hobgoblin Command.

No more! The goblins will reclaim their homes once and for all. They will rise from the obscure darkness and unite the tribes into an empire that will rule the whole of Serpentspine. By conquest, plunder, flames, explosions, and whatever other schemes can be dreamed up, it is now up to you to claim the mountains and see goblins rise as they never have before.

H95.title: The Dwarven Adventurers: Began's Expedition
H95.lore:
"Be it resolved that: 1) An expeditionary force shall be assembled, with composition as outlined in Appendix 2 ... iii) Led by Jarmun Asra ... 2) Reclamation profits shall be used as seed funding for further Serpentspine endeavours ... loan repayment shall follow Schedule IV ... Endnote: Our fiduciary duty mandates that profits must take precedence over prestige or any idea of 'Dwarven glory'."
-Fragments of the Asra Bank's charter, establishing funding for an expedition to the Serpentspine, 1443

Primer
Once, the dwarven people stretched across the entirety of the Serpentspine Mountain Range, from its westernmost borders at Escann to the farthest reaches of Yanshen. Once, all dwarf-kind was unified under the Empire of Aul-Dwarov, All-Dwarves-Home, and had experienced their golden age long before humanity built their first castles.

But that was millennia ago. Aul-Dwarov slowly crumbled, as many Holds fell to the Orcs and Goblins. The central government, the Amlharaz, collapsed with the fall of the old capital of the empire, Amldihr. The great Dwarven Rail maintained by Er-Natvir, which once carried ale from Hul-az-Krakazol and scientific discoveries from Dûr-Vazhatun across the entire Serpentspine, fell into disrepair.

The long, grim, Last Days of the Dwarovar ended when countless numbers of Orcs poured out of Hul-Jorkad. This ferocious rush across the Serpentspine shattered Holds, slaughtered dwarves, and invited even the most cowardly goblin to infest the tunnels of the mountains, leaving much of the Dwarovar in ruins.

However not every region was as devastated by the invading forces as the west had been. The Segbandal was revived in the Middle Dwarovar and thrived for millennia as the strong alliance managed to begin reclamation attempts in the west, prosper in a golden age and greatly aid the people of Bulwar. In the East Serpentspine, the Jade Empire was proclaimed by the hold of Grônstunad, claiming the title of High King and rightful heirs to the lost empire. They consolidated all the holds in their region while repelling goblins, ogres and hobgoblins from taking advantage of the chaos caused by the orcs in the west. While making enemies with the Segbandal, they managed to greatly influence the humans of Rahen and Vimdatrong and constructed the hold of Verkal Ozovar. In the end however, both the Segbandal and Jade Empire had failed in containing the menaces that beset them, leading to their falls in more recent history.

Recent History
The last Dwarven Hold to fall in the west was Khugdihr, broken by Gurukk Siegebreaker in 1424. But this was only a harbinger to the Greentide, when Korgus Dookanson unified the clans and led them pouring out of the Serpentspine into Escann and later the Deepwoods.

But the flood of orcs and goblins out of the mountains and Korgus' defeat by Corin in 1444 meant opportunity: the long-abandoned mountains were now emptier than before, and ripe for reclamation. Dwarves from the exodus states of Rubyhold and Silverforge who live amongst humanity, and the rich Asra Bank have poured funding into multiple expeditions. You are one of the many adventurers, sons and daughters of your forefathers who fled to the surface long ago, who now seek to return to the lands of your ancestors.

The road ahead will not be easy. The caverns and abandoned roads of the Dwarovrod are treacherous, and any maps have long since rotted away. Hundreds of thousands of goblins and orcs still remain, both wandering the mountains and organised into squatter states and rampaging warbands of their own. The challenges will be fierce, but ahead of you lays the ultimate prize: refounding an ancient Hold, digging deeper than ever before, and re-establishing Dwarven rule over the Serpentspine. Perhaps one day, not too long from now, Aul-Dwarov shall rise anew, even greater than before.

I01.title: The Adventurer Company: Amldihr
I01.lore:
Primer
Escann was long ago home to the first and greatest empire of humanity: Castanor. It forged a common language across the continent, and drove back both the Deepwoods as well as the fey and centaurs who lived within. However, that greatness faded after the Day of Ashen Skies, and for centuries all that remained were bickering feudal kingdoms, weakened enough to allow Castan Ebonfrost to reunify the empire in his own image when he founded the dreaded Black Castanor.

It only became darker when the 52nd Castan was made a puppet by the Sorcerer-King Nichmer, who then led Black Castanor in brutal wars until most of Cannor fell under his dominion. It would take the continent-spanning War of the Sorcerer-King, and the arrival of Elves, talented in sword and magic, to bring him down.

After Nichmer's death at the Battle of Trialmount, Castanor fractured and Escann entered its chivalric period. Kingdoms like Castellyr, Farraneán, and Adenica became lands of legend — wars were rare and conflicts were instead handled by knightly duels and jousting tournaments.

Recent History
Perhaps this practice made the region weak, as the land crumbled when the Greentide came after the fall of the dwarf hold Khugdihr in 1424. Orcs, united by Korgus Dookanson, rushed out of the Serpentspine in their millions. Castellyr was first to fall, its white walls worthless against this threat from a new direction. The knights of Adenica were slaughtered with their proud steeds, the Blademarches' legendary sword, Calindal, the Gleaming Blade was overcome, and the Marrodic were forced into their mountains.

In the west, as the Lilac Wars raged, many ignored these grim tidings. But following the war's end in 1443, many veterans marched east to confront the threat, earning the moniker "the Marcher Lords". They were joined by a plethora of adventuring companies, from as far afield as Lorent and Kheterata, as well as the Count's League, a remnant band of Castellyrian counts led by Carleon Blacktower, and a force of griffon-riders led by Prince Gryf 'the Young'.

This force dealt the Greentide some of its first defeats, waging a bloody stalemate at the Battle of Rottenstep. Among the many that fell there was Corin, a red-headed squire of Bennon — but that night, she arose as an avatar of the fallen god Agrados. Reborn and renewed, she founded the adventuring party Corin's Circle, rallied the Marcher Lords, and led them all the way to Castonath itself. There, while defending Lothane 'Bluetusk' Silmuna with her shield, and killing Dookanson with her sword, she was slain for the second time. And this time, she would not rise.

That was yesterday, the 10th of Nerament. Now, the morning after, the sun rises upon a new Escann, even if it is still infested by orcish and goblin warbands. Will you reforge one of the kingdoms of old? Or create something new? Perhaps one day all Cannor will again be reshaped by the will of an Escanni Emperor.

Primer

It forged a common language across the continent, and drove back both the Deepwoods as well as the fey and centaurs who lived within. However, that greatness faded after the Day of Ashen Skies, and for centuries all that remained were bickering feudal kingdoms, weakened enough to allow Castan Ebonfrost to reunify the empire in his own image when he founded the dreaded Black Castanor.

It only became darker when the 52nd Castan was made a puppet by the Sorcerer-King Nichmer, who then led Black Castanor in brutal wars until most of Cannor fell under his dominion. It would take the continent-spanning War of the Sorcerer-King, and the arrival of Elves, talented in sword and magic, to bring him down.

Recent History
Perhaps this practice made the region weak, as the land crumbled when the Greentide came after the fall of the dwarf hold Khugdihr in 1424. However, that greatness faded after the Day of Ashen Skies, and for centuries all that remained were bickering feudal kingdoms, weakened enough to allow Castan Ebonfrost to reunify the empire in his own image when he founded the dreaded Black Castanor.

It only became darker when the 52nd Castan was made a puppet by the Sorcerer-King Nichmer, who then led Black Castanor in brutal wars until most of Cannor fell under his dominion. It would take the continent-spanning War of the Sorcerer-King, and the arrival of Elves, talented in sword and magic, to bring him down.

After Nichmer's death at the Battle of Trialmount, Castanor fractured and Escann entered its chivalric period. Kingdoms like Castellyr, Farraneán, and Adenica became lands of legend — wars were rare and conflicts were instead handled by knightly duels and jousting tournaments.

Recent History
Perhaps this practice made the region weak, as the land crumbled when the Greentide came after the fall of the dwarf hold Khugdihr in 1424. Orcs, united by Korgus Dookanson, rushed out of the Serpentspine in their millions. Castellyr was first to fall, its white walls worthless against this threat from a new direction. The knights of Adenica were slaughtered with their proud steeds, the Blademarches' legendary sword, Calindal, the Gleaming Blade was overcome, and the Marrodic were forced into their mountains.

In the west, as the Lilac Wars raged, many ignored these grim tidings. But following the war's end in 1443, many veterans marched east to confront the threat, earning the moniker "the Marcher Lords". They were joined by a plethora of adventuring companies, from as far afield as Lorent and Kheterata, as well as the Count's League, a remnant band of Castellyrian counts led by Carleon Blacktower, and a force of griffon-riders led by Prince Gryf 'the Young'.

This force dealt the Greentide some of its first defeats, waging a bloody stalemate at the Battle of Rottenstep. Among the many that fell there was Corin, a red-headed squire of Bennon — but that night, she arose as an avatar of the fallen god Agrados. Reborn and renewed, she founded the adventuring party Corin's Circle, rallied the Marcher Lords, and led them all the way to Castonath itself. There, while defending Lothane 'Bluetusk' Silmuna with her shield, and killing Dookanson with her sword, she was slain for the second time. And this time, she would not rise.

That was yesterday, the 10th of Nerament. Now, the morning after, the sun rises upon a new Escann, even if it is still infested by orcish and goblin warbands. Will you reforge one of the kingdoms of old? Or create something new? Perhaps one day all Cannor will again be reshaped by the will of an Escanni Emperor.

I26.title: The Disparate Wood Elf Clans: Salla Elyn
I26.lore:
"When our forebears arrived in this forest, were they not one people? Were they not united under one cause? Under one banner? Should we, their children, not seek wisdom from that?"
- Narawen Drennen

Recent History
For the Wood Elves, a thousand years have passed since their Moon Elf ancestors became trapped behind the Verdant Veil, their flanking maneuver against the Sorcerer-King Nichmer going terribly wrong. Stranded within the forest, the elves adapted to their new home, learning to cope with the ways of the Fey Courts and squabbling with the Forest Goblins over territory. Over the centuries, they settled the forest groves and fragmented into distinct and independent salla - Wood Elvish for "clans". 

The deeply fragmented and decentralized salla were not ready for the Greentide. When it arrived in the Deepwoods, it scattered the elven defenders and saw whole clans destroyed and enslaved, while others were pushed to the edges of their ancestral groves. It seemed as though the Wood Elves would be lost in this calamity - Thyr Gaethneán, "The Whirlwind."

Yet against this storm, the remaining heads of the clans held firm, refusing to allow their people to be swept away. Putting aside generations of petty quarrels, these leaders traveled to the city of Elynáile and, in a rare act of unity forged the Verdant Pact, an alliance to coordinate, defend, and reclaim their homes.

Now, for the first time since its arrival, Elvenkind in the Deepwoods is united in one singular purpose. Whether that will be enough to retake their homeland and drive back the invaders is yet to be seen.

When it arrived in the Deepwoods, it scattered the elven defenders and saw whole clans destroyed and enslaved, while others were pushed to the edges of their ancestral groves. It seemed as though the Wood Elves would be lost in this calamity - Thyr Gaethneán, "The Whirlwind."

Yet against this storm, the remaining heads of the clans held firm, refusing to allow their people to be swept away. Putting aside generations of petty quarrels, these leaders traveled to the city of Elynáile and, in a rare act of unity forged the Verdant Pact, an alliance to coordinate, defend, and reclaim their homes.

Now, for the first time since its arrival, Elvenkind in the Deepwoods is united in one singular purpose. Whether that will be enough to retake their homeland and drive back the invaders is yet to be seen.

I31.title: The Disparate Wood Elf Clans: Salla Scotha
I31.lore:
"When our forebears arrived in this forest, were they not one people? Were they not united under one cause? Under one banner? Should we, their children, not seek wisdom from that?"
- Narawen Drennen

Recent History
For the Wood Elves, a thousand years have passed since their Moon Elf ancestors became trapped behind the Verdant Veil, their flanking maneuver against the Sorcerer-King Nichmer going terribly wrong. Stranded within the forest, the elves adapted to their new home, learning to cope with the ways of the Fey Courts and squabbling with the Forest Goblins over territory. Over the centuries, they settled the forest groves and fragmented into distinct and independent salla - Wood Elvish for "clans".

I35.title: The Resurgent Forest Goblins: Brambleskinner
I35.lore:
"Forest Goblin, Outsider Goblin. What’s the difference? Everyone out there wants you enslaved or dead. Do you think they care whether our eyes glow yellow or not?" - Cleesb Spiked Log

Recent History
Goblins have lived in the Deepwoods for much, much longer than orcs or elves. Their civilization had long passed its zenith by the time the Wood Elves arrived, an event that broke what little control they had left over the Deepwoods. Consigned to the dark corners of the groves and glades, the Forest Goblin tribes endured, consigned to obscurity.

Then, three things happened that drastically and irrevocably changed the Deepwoods. First, the goblin warlord Oubblig won a string of wars that saw much of the southwest Deepwoods fall under goblin rule. Then, an immense invasion of orcs tore into the Deepwoods from the north, defeating many of the elven clans and bringing many goblin slaves along with them. Finally, the Verdant Veil fell, once again opening travel out of the Deepwoods. With the elves and orcs at each other’s throats, many of the enslaved goblins escaped and joined our ranks, and with the outside world now open to us, there has never been an opportunity like this for the goblins of the Deepwoods.

Still there is division. Oubblig is dead, his diminished realm split between Oubbligschild and Brambleskinner. Verminhusk is utterly surrounded by orcish tribes, while the Spiked Log clan’s acceptance of many runaway slaves has led Relgakk Blackmoon to vow to hunt down every last goblin in the Deepwoods. Goblins are still outnumbered by orcs and elves alike, and the old rivalry between the Pactseeker goblins and those that follow the Shaman Old Ways has far from disappeared. But even with these desperate conditions, this may be the day when the goblins rise to greatness.

I38.title: The Receding Deepwoods Greentide: Beasteater
I38.lore:
"Korgus is dead? What does that matter to us, stranded here in these damned woods? What help has the Dookanson been to us here? …Dookan’s guidance is far away. He will find us again… or we will carve our own path." - Relgakk Blackmoon

Recent History
The orcs are new arrivals to the Deepwoods. It was only when the Greentide swept into Escann in the 1420s that the first orcs even entered the forest, and those who did were few in number. The woods’ inhabitants hardly gave the first stranded stragglers a thought, but in 1439 AA the full extent of orcish might would be felt when the great clans of Silentblade, Blackmoon, and Cursed Howl launched a massive expedition into the forest. The Greentide Invasion of the Deepwoods devastated the Wood Elves and Forest Goblins, with whole clans destroyed and the rest left on the brink of collapse.

These initial battlefield successes would quickly give way to infighting when the great clans learned of the Verdant Veil, the magical trap that kept any mortal from leaving the Deepwoods. Word quickly spread in the orcish camp that the horde was trapped, and while the chieftains worked together in an effort to escape, their efforts were for naught.

As their imprisonment became apparent and the cost of the War in the Wood mounted, orcish unity shattered. Clan Cursed Howl would be the first to leave the horde, led by honor-bound Gordak Cursed Howl to settle the Flower Grove. Soon after, the Silentblade abruptly broke off from their allies and settled into the Shadow Grove, their chieftain Troknar Silentblade giving little explanation for his decision. Relgakk Blackmoon and the remaining orcs would push into the Arrow Grove before exhaustion and a goblin slave revolt finally quashed any hope of escape.

Ironically, the Verdant Veil fell soon after the Deepwoods Greentide had shattered. The shocking news of Korgus Dookanson’s death was met with general apathy from the clans, who had long since been cut off from his orders. What mattered now was surviving their future in the dark forest they had come to call home…

I47.title: The Forbidden Plains, the Centaur Clans: Northern Wind
I47.lore:
"Tens of thousands of Centaurs swarmed across the city-states of the Triunic Lakes, watering the trampled crops with the blood of their former caretakers. None were spared or given the slightest hint of mercy. And thus, an entire generation was lost."
-A Historic Account of the Great Incursion, by Raanu Modsuu (1511 AA)

Primer
The Centaurs of the Forbidden Plains are ancient creatures, originally being Fey, having existed on Halann before the start of recorded history. In fact, the Centaurs today no longer live in their homeland, the Deepwoods in Escann, which used to stretch all the way to the Greatwoods in Gawed.

It was Castan Beastbane who mercilessly slaughtered most of the non-human races in Cannor from 596 to 569 B.A.. Like the Satyrs and Gnolls, the Centaurs almost went extinct in the Second Great Cleansing. However, survivors managed to escape to the Forbidden Plains, where they remain today. Their homeland is no longer recognisable, the majority of the woods having been burnt down.

On the plains, Centaurs have tried to establish a new homeland for themselves, pushing the humans who lived on the mainland to the lakes, where their descendants have formed the Triunic peoples.

Recent History
The Centaurs are typically a quarrelsome race, only having been united twice by Great Caehns during their history on the plains. Both times, the clans have refractured due to their defeat at the hands of the Triunic city-states.

The most recent and most devastating invasion was the Great Incursion, which began in 1433 A.A. and ended in 1440 A.A. The Centaurs took advantage of ancient Fey magic, with the use of Centaur shamans and the most frigid and harsh winter in Triunic history. The shamans locked ice bridges in place, allowing Centaurs to swarm over the sea and attack the humans. However, the clever humans found out about the shamans, and used any means necessary to slay them.
