Like the Satyrs and Gnolls, the Centaurs almost went extinct in the Second Great Cleansing. However, survivors managed to escape to the Forbidden Plains, where they remain today. Their homeland is no longer recognisable, the majority of the woods having been burnt down.

On the plains, Centaurs have tried to establish a new homeland for themselves, pushing the humans who lived on the mainland to the lakes, where their descendants have formed the Triunic peoples.

Recent History
The Centaurs are typically a quarrelsome race, only having been united twice by Great Caehns during their history on the plains. Both times, the clans have refractured due to their defeat at the hands of the Triunic city-states.

The most recent and most devastating invasion was the Great Incursion, which began in 1433 A.A. and ended in 1440 A.A. The Centaurs took advantage of ancient Fey magic, with the use of Centaur shamans and the most frigid and harsh winter in Triunic history. The shamans locked ice bridges in place, allowing Centaurs to swarm over the sea and attack the humans. However, the clever humans found out about the shamans, and used any means necessary to slay them. In 1439 A.A., the last shaman was killed and the invasion slowly dissipated as the ice bridges melted, causing the remaining warriors to be slaughtered.

Now, you lead one of many Centaur clans, dreaming to unify your people once more. However, the humans have signed a treaty of unity, joining together in the ambitious Lake Federation. O Great Caehn, you must crush these foolish dreams below the thunderous tremor of hundreds of thousands of hooves. Will you unite the clans and conquer once more? Or will your wars cause a Third Great Cleansing? Or perhaps you will succeed... only to fall apart as a war lasts a little too long?

May your enemies tremble before you and lay dying in regret for their savage actions in annihilating your kind. The storm shall never end!

I83.lore:
"Tens of thousands of Centaurs swarmed across the city-states of the Triunic Lakes, watering the trampled crops with the blood of their former caretakers. None were spared or given the slightest hint of mercy. And thus, an entire generation was lost."
-A Historic Account of the Great Incursion, by Raanu Modsuu (1511 AA)

Primer
The Centaurs of the Forbidden Plains are ancient creatures, originally being Fey, having existed on Halann before the start of recorded history. In fact, the Centaurs today no longer live in their homeland, the Deepwoods in Escann, which used to stretch all the way to the Greatwoods in Gawed.

It was Castan Beastbane who mercilessly slaughtered most of the non-human races in Cannor from 596 to 569 B.A.. Like the Satyrs and Gnolls, the Centaurs almost went extinct in the Second Great Cleansing. However, survivors managed to escape to the Forbidden Plains, where they remain today. Their homeland is no longer recognisable, the majority of the woods having been burnt down.

On the plains, Centaurs have tried to establish a new homeland for themselves, pushing the humans who lived on the mainland to the lakes, where their descendants have formed the Triunic peoples.

Recent History
The Centaurs are typically a quarrelsome race, only having been united twice by Great Caehns during their history on the plains. Both times, the clans have refractured due to their defeat at the hands of the Triunic city-states.

The most recent and most devastating invasion was the Great Incursion, which began in 1433 A.A. and ended in 1440 A.A. The Centaurs took advantage of ancient Fey magic, with the use of Centaur shamans and the most frigid and harsh winter in Triunic history. The shamans locked ice bridges in place, allowing Centaurs to swarm over the sea and attack the humans. However, the clever humans found out about the shamans, and used any means necessary to slay them. In 1439 A.A., the last shaman was killed and the invasion slowly dissipated as the ice bridges melted, causing the remaining warriors to be slaughtered.

Now, you lead one of many Centaur clans, dreaming to unify your people once more. However, the humans have signed a treaty of unity, joining together in the ambitious Lake Federation. O Great Caehn, you must crush these foolish dreams below the thunderous tremor of hundreds of thousands of hooves. Will you unite the clans and conquer once more? Or will your wars cause a Third Great Cleansing? Or perhaps you will succeed... only to fall apart as a war lasts a little too long?

I90.title: The Forbidden Plains, Ogre Kingdoms: Maghargma
I90.lore:
Primer
The Ogres, along with Trolls, are the last of the Giantkin in Halann. The True Giants were one of the original races to inhabit the world, eons ago, but fell in a mutually destructive conflict with the Dragons well before the Day of Ashen Skies. The Ogres claim that they devoured the True Giants and as such gained their strength and power; these cannibalistic beliefs continue to influence their religion (the Feast of the Gods) and culture.

Ogres have long roamed the Forbidden Plains, though the Great Caehn Neagol's conquest of the central steppes coupled with climactic changes after the Day of Ashen Skies led to the Sandmaw Ogres being driven extinct and the Fathide Ogres pushed to the western hills. In AA 28, with the Fathides seemed poised to meet the Sandmaws' fate, their destiny was forever reshaped by the crash-landing of a Sky Giant Fortress into their hills. The inhabitants had perished, and the Ogres gladly feasted on their corpses, regaining their strength and allowing them to survive the long famine years. The chief of the Gravelhide clan, Ghornma Gravelhide, subjugated all the other clans that flocked to the ruins, and formed the kingdom of Maghargma.

Recent History
For centuries, the dominant Ogre Kingdom has remained Maghargma in the south, which makes its capital in the old Sky Giant fortress. In its prime the Kingdom extorted tribute from dozens of other Great Clans, along with a plethora of Centaur tribes who brought slaves and food in return for boons of iron and steel. The northern tribes, however, have remained rebellious ever since AA 125, when Roghrin Gravelhide's rivals fled there following his victory in the Maghargma civil war. The states closer to Maghargma's power base remain loyal, though some of the outlying Great Clans merely pay lip service to their sovereign.

In 1440, however, the same Centaur invasion that forced the Triunic Peoples in the East to unify into the Lake Federation in defense also struck westward, and slew Maghargma's ruler Borigg Gravelhide in battle. His son Ziltagh Gravelhide seized the throne, but he was not another in the long line of Maghargma's rulers who were content to merely sit on their throne and eat. A massive ogre, who crushed centaurs whole with a single blow during his counteroffensive against the Hordes' incursion, his rise has been a check to any rebellious tendencies amongst other Ogres.

Still, every Ogre knows one truth above all: the world is eat or be eaten. Will Ziltagh be able to re-establish Maghargma's dominance, quite literally devour the Forbidden Plains, and even crown himself Mengurkha, ruler of all Ogres? Or will one of their vassals consume the fading state and take up its mantle? Or shall it be one of the Wild Clans in the North, who look south with a blend of fear and ambition and wait for the chance to take a bite—or several—of their own, that shall claim dominance and form Surkokli? The future of the Ogres, and of the entire Forbidden Plains, is up to you.

The shamans locked ice bridges in place, allowing Centaurs to swarm over the sea and attack the humans. However, the clever humans found out about the shamans, and used any means necessary to slay them. In 1439 A.A., the last shaman was killed and the invasion slowly dissipated as the ice bridges melted, causing the remaining warriors to be slaughtered.

Now, you lead one of many Centaur clans, dreaming to unify your people once more. However, the humans have signed a treaty of unity, joining together in the ambitious Lake Federation. O Great Caehn, you must crush these foolish dreams below the thunderous tremor of hundreds of thousands of hooves. Will you unite the clans and conquer once more? Or will your wars cause a Third Great Cleansing? Or perhaps you will succeed... only to fall apart as a war lasts a little too long?

May your enemies tremble before you and lay dying in regret for their savage actions in annihilating your kind. The storm shall never end!

I95.title: The Forbidden Plains, the Centaur Clans: Raging Blizzard
I95.lore:
"Tens of thousands of Centaurs swarmed across the city-states of the Triunic Lakes, watering the trampled crops with the blood of their former caretakers. None were spared or given the slightest hint of mercy. And thus, an entire generation was lost."
-A Historic Account of the Great Incursion, by Raanu Modsuu (1511 AA)

Primer
The Centaurs of the Forbidden Plains are ancient creatures, originally being Fey, having existed on Halann before the start of recorded history. In fact, the Centaurs today no longer live in their homeland, the Deepwoods in Escann, which used to stretch all the way to the Greatwoods in Gawed.

It was Castan Beastbane who mercilessly slaughtered most of the non-human races in Cannor from 596 to 569 B.A.. Like the Satyrs and Gnolls, the Centaurs almost went extinct in the Second Great Cleansing. However, survivors managed to escape to the Forbidden Plains, where they remain today. Their homeland is no longer recognisable, the majority of the woods having been burnt down.

On the plains, Centaurs have tried to establish a new homeland for themselves, pushing the humans who lived on the mainland to the lakes, where their descendants have formed the Triunic peoples.

Recent History
The Centaurs are typically a quarrelsome race, only having been united twice by Great Caehns during their history on the plains. Both times, the clans have refractured due to their defeat at the hands of the Triunic city-states.

J01.title: The Lake Federation, Cities of the North: Kovtalzar
J01.lore:
"We, the people of the Triunic Lakes, agree to henceforth join together in the Lake Federation to bind our republics closer together for the greater good."
-Preamble to the Lake Federation Founding Document.

Primer
The people of the Triunic Lakes are a relatively young civilisation compared to the rest of the world. Originally a group of nomadic tribes, the Khamgunai people first arrived on the lakes in 680 BA. A hundred years later, more humans would immigrate to the region due to raging hordes of Centaurs, who fled to Forbidden Plains after Castan II Beastbane burned down most of the Deepwoods.

The Triunic Lakes consists of plenty of arable land and some of the most fertile soil in the world, allowing it to easily maintain a population of millions of people. However, it wasn't until the Day of Ashen Skies that most of the remaining humans on the mainland migrated to the lakes and established more sedentary lifestyles.

The Triunic Lakes are divided into three cultures: the Khamgunai (the original settlers of the land), the Metsamic (tending to themselves in fortified cities), and the Zabatlari (who have begun to dominate the economy).

There are also three religions in the region, each representing a lake which in turn represents a goddess: Kodave, Yukel, and Enuuk.

The Tyrant Queen
Long ago, before the Day of Ashen Skies, the lands of the lakes were ruled not by its people, but by tyrants. Kings lived in opulence as the people begged for scraps, lords danced in ballrooms as villages burned to ash, and the robber barons squeezed every last clink of coin. But in the year 274 BA came the beginning to the end. A woman, queen and mage, rose to power. Her path to the throne was marred by blood, and greed. Her wars against the other cities left a wake of destruction and misery across the lands, with spoils looted and captives dragged back to her spires in black.

When her ambitions came to light, a desire to achievement Immortality at any cost, the leaders and nobility did not rise to stop her. Instead, the lords sold out their own people, and the mages happily complied for just a chance at her tutelage. It was the common man, ignited by injustice, who united against her. As her armies lost their ground, she unleashed a horde of twisted monstrosities. Their screams were that of the loved, and the lost. The City of Tyranny, from where she ruled, was put to the torch.

Countless were brought to justice as the pyres were lit, and the fires burned bright. Bloodlines ended, and magic all but ceased in these lands. Though many claimed innocence, the trust between the magical and mundane had been shattered. But while dynasties could end, magic seemed like an inevitable force, one which would perpetually remerge. As the bloodlust subsided, the cracks in our methods began to show, a question pierced through the conscience of all the peoples of the lakes: were all mages truly guilty? Could a newborn babe truly bear the burden, the guilt, of its forefathers?

This question has remained with the people of the Triunic Lakes ever since.

Recent History
The Lake Federation is in its infancy, only being formed in 1441. While there were previous precedents of unity such as the Amberpact, none were as ambitious or as encompassing as the federation, which attempted to finally end the petty internal squabbles of the lakes. However, this was only due to the recent Centaur invasion, which happened to be the largest and most devastating in Triunic history.

Nicknamed the Great Incursion, the Centaur Shamans made use of the harshest winter ever recorded by locking the frozen lakes in place and using them as ice bridges, allowing huge centaur hordes to invade, wreaking havoc across the south. The path of blood finally ended in 1440 with the hunting of the last shamans in 1439.

You lead a city-state in the north, the primary supporters of the Lake Federation. With the scars of the previous decade still largely visible in the lands of your southern compatriots and missing men from your fields, the foundation of the federation is shaky at best. Be aware of any crises that may come, as many city-states such as Vahjevgiiv seek to expand their own domain for their own greed. Keep tabs on the Metsamic city-states, or the federation may well collapse. And still, its only a matter of time before the centaurs reorganise and reap a new path of blood once more...

The federation awaits your rule. Will you manage to unify the Triunic Lakes? Or will the city-states burn once more, either by their own hands or under the hooves of the centaurs?

A hundred years later, more humans would immigrate to the region due to raging hordes of Centaurs, who fled to Forbidden Plains after Castan II Beastbane burned down most of the Deepwoods.

The Triunic Lakes consists of plenty of arable land and some of the most fertile soil in the world, allowing it to easily maintain a population of millions of people. However, it wasn't until the Day of Ashen Skies that most of the remaining humans on the mainland migrated to the lakes and established more sedentary lifestyles.

The Triunic Lakes are divided into three cultures: the Khamgunai (the original settlers of the land), the Metsamic (tending to themselves in fortified cities), and the Zabatlari (who have begun to dominate the economy).

There are also three religions in the region, each representing a lake which in turn represents a goddess: Kodave, Yukel, and Enuuk.

The Tyrant Queen
Long ago, before the Day of Ashen Skies, the lands of the lakes were ruled not by its people, but by tyrants. Kings lived in opulence as the people begged for scraps, lords danced in ballrooms as villages burned to ash, and the robber barons squeezed every last clink of coin. But in the year 274 BA came the beginning to the end. A woman, queen and mage, rose to power. Her path to the throne was marred by blood, and greed. Her wars against the other cities left a wake of destruction and misery across the lands, with spoils looted and captives dragged back to her spires in black.

When her ambitions came to light, a desire to achievement Immortality at any cost, the leaders and nobility did not rise to stop her. Instead, the lords sold out their own people, and the mages happily complied for just a chance at her tutelage. It was the common man, ignited by injustice, who united against her. As her armies lost their ground, she unleashed a horde of twisted monstrosities. Their screams were that of the loved, and the lost. The City of Tyranny, from where she ruled, was put to the torch.

Countless were brought to justice as the pyres were lit, and the fires burned bright. Bloodlines ended, and magic all but ceased in these lands. Though many claimed innocence, the trust between the magical and mundane had been shattered. But while dynasties could end, magic seemed like an inevitable force, one which would perpetually remerge. As the bloodlust subsided, the cracks in our methods began to show, a question pierced through the conscience of all the peoples of the lakes: were all mages truly guilty? Could a newborn babe truly bear the burden, the guilt, of its forefathers?

This question has remained with the people of the Triunic Lakes ever since.

Recent History
The Lake Federation is in its infancy, only being formed in 1441. While there were previous precedents of unity such as the Amberpact, none were as ambitious or as encompassing as the federation, which attempted to finally end the petty internal squabbles of the lakes. However, this was only due to the recent Centaur invasion, which happened to be the largest and most devastating in Triunic history.

Nicknamed the Great Incursion, the Centaur Shamans made use of the harshest winter ever recorded by locking the frozen lakes in place and using them as ice bridges, allowing huge centaur hordes to invade, wreaking havoc across the south. The path of blood finally ended in 1440 with the hunting of the last shamans in 1439.

You lead a city-state in the north, the primary supporters of the Lake Federation. With the scars of the previous decade still largely visible in the lands of your southern compatriots and missing men from your fields, the foundation of the federation is shaky at best. Be aware of any crises that may come, as many city-states such as Vahjevgiiv seek to expand their own domain for their own greed. Keep tabs on the Metsamic city-states, or the federation may well collapse. And still, its only a matter of time before the centaurs reorganise and reap a new path of blood once more...

The federation awaits your rule. Will you manage to unify the Triunic Lakes? Or will the city-states burn once more, either by their own hands or under the hooves of the centaurs?

There are also three religions in the region, each representing a lake which in turn represents a goddess: Kodave, Yukel, and Enuuk.

The Tyrant Queen
Long ago, before the Day of Ashen Skies, the lands of the lakes were ruled not by its people, but by tyrants. Kings lived in opulence as the people begged for scraps, lords danced in ballrooms as villages burned to ash, and the robber barons squeezed every last clink of coin. But in the year 274 BA came the beginning to the end. A woman, queen and mage, rose to power. Her path to the throne was marred by blood, and greed. Her wars against the other cities left a wake of destruction and misery across the lands, with spoils looted and captives dragged back to her spires in black.

When her ambitions came to light, a desire to achievement Immortality at any cost, the leaders and nobility did not rise to stop her. Instead, the lords sold out their own people, and the mages happily complied for just a chance at her tutelage. It was the common man, ignited by injustice, who united against her. As her armies lost their ground, she unleashed a horde of twisted monstrosities. Their screams were that of the loved, and the lost. The City of Tyranny, from where she ruled, was put to the torch.

Countless were brought to justice as the pyres were lit, and the fires burned bright. Bloodlines ended, and magic all but ceased in these lands. Though many claimed innocence, the trust between the magical and mundane had been shattered. But while dynasties could end, magic seemed like an inevitable force, one which would perpetually remerge. As the bloodlust subsided, the cracks in our methods began to show, a question pierced through the conscience of all the peoples of the lakes: were all mages truly guilty? Could a newborn babe truly bear the burden, the guilt, of its forefathers?

This question has remained with the people of the Triunic Lakes ever since.

Recent History
The Lake Federation is in its infancy, only being formed in 1441. While there were previous precedents of unity such as the Amberpact, none were as ambitious or as encompassing as the federation, which attempted to finally end the petty internal squabbles of the lakes. However, this was only due to the recent Centaur invasion, which happened to be the largest and most devastating in Triunic history.

Nicknamed the Great Incursion, the Centaur Shamans made use of the harshest winter ever recorded by locking the frozen lakes in place and using them as ice bridges, allowing huge centaur hordes to invade, wreaking havoc across the south. The path of blood finally ended in 1440 with the hunting of the last shamans in 1439.

You lead a city-state in the north, the primary supporters of the Lake Federation. With the scars of the previous decade still largely visible in the lands of your southern compatriots and missing men from your fields, the foundation of the federation is shaky at best. Be aware of any crises that may come, as many city-states such as Vahjevgiiv seek to expand their own domain for their own greed. Keep tabs on the Metsamic city-states, or the federation may well collapse. And still, its only a matter of time before the centaurs reorganise and reap a new path of blood once more...

The federation awaits your rule. Will you manage to unify the Triunic Lakes? Or will the city-states burn once more, either by their own hands or under the hooves of the centaurs?

Her wars against the other cities left a wake of destruction and misery across the lands, with spoils looted and captives dragged back to her spires in black.

When her ambitions came to light, a desire to achievement Immortality at any cost, the leaders and nobility did not rise to stop her. Instead, the lords sold out their own people, and the mages happily complied for just a chance at her tutelage. It was the common man, ignited by injustice, who united against her. As her armies lost their ground, she unleashed a horde of twisted monstrosities. Their screams were that of the loved, and the lost. The City of Tyranny, from where she ruled, was put to the torch.

Countless were brought to justice as the pyres were lit, and the fires burned bright. Bloodlines ended, and magic all but ceased in these lands. Though many claimed innocence, the trust between the magical and mundane had been shattered. But while dynasties could end, magic seemed like an inevitable force, one which would perpetually remerge. As the bloodlust subsided, the cracks in our methods began to show, a question pierced through the conscience of all the peoples of the lakes: were all mages truly guilty? Could a newborn babe truly bear the burden, the guilt, of its forefathers?

This question has remained with the people of the Triunic Lakes ever since.

Recent History
The Lake Federation is in its infancy, only being formed in 1441. While there were previous precedents of unity such as the Amberpact, none were as ambitious or as encompassing as the federation, which attempted to finally end the petty internal squabbles of the lakes. However, this was only due to the recent Centaur invasion, which happened to be the largest and most devastating in Triunic history.

Nicknamed the Great Incursion, the Centaur Shamans made use of the harshest winter ever recorded by locking the frozen lakes in place and using them as ice bridges, allowing huge centaur hordes to invade, wreaking havoc across the south. The path of blood finally ended in 1440 with the hunting of the last shamans in 1439.

You lead a city-state in the north, the primary supporters of the Lake Federation. With the scars of the previous decade still largely visible in the lands of your southern compatriots and missing men from your fields, the foundation of the federation is shaky at best. Be aware of any crises that may come, as many city-states such as Vahjevgiiv seek to expand their own domain for their own greed. Keep tabs on the Metsamic city-states, or the federation may well collapse. And still, its only a matter of time before the centaurs reorganise and reap a new path of blood once more...

The federation awaits your rule. Will you manage to unify the Triunic Lakes? Or will the city-states burn once more, either by their own hands or under the hooves of the centaurs?

As her armies lost their ground, she unleashed a horde of twisted monstrosities. Their screams were that of the loved, and the lost. The City of Tyranny, from where she ruled, was put to the torch.

Countless were brought to justice as the pyres were lit, and the fires burned bright. Bloodlines ended, and magic all but ceased in these lands. Though many claimed innocence, the trust between the magical and mundane had been shattered. But while dynasties could end, magic seemed like an inevitable force, one which would perpetually remerge. As the bloodlust subsided, the cracks in our methods began to show, a question pierced through the conscience of all the peoples of the lakes: were all mages truly guilty? Could a newborn babe truly bear the burden, the guilt, of its forefathers?

This question has remained with the people of the Triunic Lakes ever since.

Recent History
The Lake Federation is in its infancy, only being formed in 1441. While there were previous precedents of unity such as the Amberpact, none were as ambitious or as encompassing as the federation, which attempted to finally end the petty internal squabbles of the lakes. However, this was only due to the recent Centaur invasion, which happened to be the largest and most devastating in Triunic history.

Nicknamed the Great Incursion, the Centaur Shamans made use of the harshest winter ever recorded by locking the frozen lakes in place and using them as ice bridges, allowing huge centaur hordes to invade, wreaking havoc across the south. The path of blood finally ended in 1440 with the hunting of the last shamans in 1439.

You lead a city-state in the north, the primary supporters of the Lake Federation. With the scars of the previous decade still largely visible in the lands of your southern compatriots and missing men from your fields, the foundation of the federation is shaky at best. Be aware of any crises that may come, as many city-states such as Vahjevgiiv seek to expand their own domain for their own greed. Keep tabs on the Metsamic city-states, or the federation may well collapse. And still, its only a matter of time before the centaurs reorganise and reap a new path of blood once more...

The federation awaits your rule. Will you manage to unify the Triunic Lakes? Or will the city-states burn once more, either by their own hands or under the hooves of the centaurs?

J32.title: The Forbidden Plains, Ogre Kingdoms: Mire Maw
J32.lore:
Primer
The Ogres, along with Trolls, are the last of the Giantkin in Halann. The True Giants were one of the original races to inhabit the world, eons ago, but fell in a mutually destructive conflict with the Dragons well before the Day of Ashen Skies. The Ogres claim that they devoured the True Giants and as such gained their strength and power; these cannibalistic beliefs continue to influence their religion (the Feast of the Gods) and culture.

Ogres have long roamed the Forbidden Plains, though the Great Caehn Neagol's conquest of the central steppes coupled with climactic changes after the Day of Ashen Skies led to the Sandmaw Ogres being driven extinct and the Fathide Ogres pushed to the western hills. In AA 28, with the Fathides seemed poised to meet the Sandmaws' fate, their destiny was forever reshaped by the crash-landing of a Sky Giant Fortress into their hills. The inhabitants had perished, and the Ogres gladly feasted on their corpses, regaining their strength and allowing them to survive the long famine years. The chief of the Gravelhide clan, Ghornma Gravelhide, subjugated all the other clans that flocked to the ruins, and formed the kingdom of Maghargma.

Recent History
For centuries, the dominant Ogre Kingdom has remained Maghargma in the south, which makes its capital in the old Sky Giant fortress. In its prime the Kingdom extorted tribute from dozens of other Great Clans, along with a plethora of Centaur tribes who brought slaves and food in return for boons of iron and steel. The northern tribes, however, have remained rebellious ever since AA 125, when Roghrin Gravelhide's rivals fled there following his victory in the Maghargma civil war. The states closer to Maghargma's power base remain loyal, though some of the outlying Great Clans merely pay lip service to their sovereign.

In 1440, however, the same Centaur invasion that forced the Triunic Peoples in the East to unify into the Lake Federation in defense also struck westward, and slew Maghargma's ruler Borigg Gravelhide in battle. His son Ziltagh Gravelhide seized the throne, but he was not another in the long line of Maghargma's rulers who were content to merely sit on their throne and eat. A massive ogre, who crushed centaurs whole with a single blow during his counteroffensive against the Hordes' incursion, his rise has been a check to any rebellious tendencies amongst other Ogres.

Still, every Ogre knows one truth above all: the world is eat or be eaten. Will Ziltagh be able to re-establish Maghargma's dominance, quite literally devour the Forbidden Plains, and even crown himself Mengurkha, ruler of all Ogres? Or will one of their vassals consume the fading state and take up its mantle? Or shall it be one of the Wild Clans in the North, who look south with a blend of fear and ambition and wait for the chance to take a bite—or several—of their own, that shall claim dominance and form Surkokli? The future of the Ogres, and of the entire Forbidden Plains, is up to you.

The states closer to Maghargma's power base remain loyal, though some of the outlying Great Clans merely pay lip service to their sovereign.

In 1440, however, the same Centaur invasion that forced the Triunic Peoples in the East to unify into the Lake Federation in defense also struck westward, and slew Maghargma's ruler Borigg Gravelhide in battle. His son Ziltagh Gravelhide seized the throne, but he was not another in the long line of Maghargma's rulers who were content to merely sit on their throne and eat. A massive ogre, who crushed centaurs whole with a single blow during his counteroffensive against the Hordes' incursion, his rise has been a check to any rebellious tendencies amongst other Ogres.

Still, every Ogre knows one truth above all: the world is eat or be eaten. Will Ziltagh be able to re-establish Maghargma's dominance, quite literally devour the Forbidden Plains, and even crown himself Mengurkha, ruler of all Ogres? Or will one of their vassals consume the fading state and take up its mantle? Or shall it be one of the Wild Clans in the North, who look south with a blend of fear and ambition and wait for the chance to take a bite—or several—of their own, that shall claim dominance and form Surkokli? The future of the Ogres, and of the entire Forbidden Plains, is up to you.

His son Ziltagh Gravelhide seized the throne, but he was not another in the long line of Maghargma's rulers who were content to merely sit on their throne and eat. A massive ogre, who crushed centaurs whole with a single blow during his counteroffensive against the Hordes' incursion, his rise has been a check to any rebellious tendencies amongst other Ogres.

Still, every Ogre knows one truth above all: the world is eat or be eaten. Will Ziltagh be able to re-establish Maghargma's dominance, quite literally devour the Forbidden Plains, and even crown himself Mengurkha, ruler of all Ogres? Or will one of their vassals consume the fading state and take up its mantle? Or shall it be one of the Wild Clans in the North, who look south with a blend of fear and ambition and wait for the chance to take a bite—or several—of their own, that shall claim dominance and form Surkokli? The future of the Ogres, and of the entire Forbidden Plains, is up to you.

J47.title: The Forbidden Plains, Ogre Kingdoms: Skurkokli
J47.lore:
Primer
The Ogres, along with Trolls, are the last of the Giantkin in Halann. The True Giants were one of the original races to inhabit the world, eons ago, but fell in a mutually destructive conflict with the Dragons well before the Day of Ashen Skies. The Ogres claim that they devoured the True Giants and as such gained their strength and power; these cannibalistic beliefs continue to influence their religion (the Feast of the Gods) and culture.

Ogres have long roamed the Forbidden Plains, though the Great Caehn Neagol's conquest of the central steppes coupled with climactic changes after the Day of Ashen Skies led to the Sandmaw Ogres being driven extinct and the Fathide Ogres pushed to the western hills. In AA 28, with the Fathides seemed poised to meet the Sandmaws' fate, their destiny was forever reshaped by the crash-landing of a Sky Giant Fortress into their hills. The inhabitants had perished, and the Ogres gladly feasted on their corpses, regaining their strength and allowing them to survive the long famine years. The chief of the Gravelhide clan, Ghornma Gravelhide, subjugated all the other clans that flocked to the ruins, and formed the kingdom of Maghargma.

Recent History
For centuries, the dominant Ogre Kingdom has remained Maghargma in the south, which makes its capital in the old Sky Giant fortress. In its prime the Kingdom extorted tribute from dozens of other Great Clans, along with a plethora of Centaur tribes who brought slaves and food in return for boons of iron and steel. The northern tribes, however, have remained rebellious ever since AA 125, when Roghrin Gravelhide's rivals fled there following his victory in the Maghargma civil war. The states closer to Maghargma's power base remain loyal, though some of the outlying Great Clans merely pay lip service to their sovereign.

His son Ziltagh Gravelhide seized the throne, but he was not another in the long line of Maghargma's rulers who were content to merely sit on their throne and eat. A massive ogre, who crushed centaurs whole with a single blow during his counteroffensive against the Hordes' incursion, his rise has been a check to any rebellious tendencies amongst other Ogres.

Still, every Ogre knows one truth above all: the world is eat or be eaten. J49.title: The Lake Federation, Cities of the North: Qorondulyuz
J49.lore:
"We, the people of the Triunic Lakes, agree to henceforth join together in the Lake Federation to bind our republics closer together for the greater good."
-Preamble to the Lake Federation Founding Document.

Primer
The people of the Triunic Lakes are a relatively young civilisation compared to the rest of the world. Originally a group of nomadic tribes, the Khamgunai people first arrived on the lakes in 680 BA. A hundred years later, more humans would immigrate to the region due to raging hordes of Centaurs, who fled to Forbidden Plains after Castan II Beastbane burned down most of the Deepwoods.

The Triunic Lakes consists of plenty of arable land and some of the most fertile soil in the world, allowing it to easily maintain a population of millions of people. However, it wasn't until the Day of Ashen Skies that most of the remaining humans on the mainland migrated to the lakes and established more sedentary lifestyles.

The Triunic Lakes are divided into three cultures: the Khamgunai (the original settlers of the land), the Metsamic (tending to themselves in fortified cities), and the Zabatlari (who have begun to dominate the economy).

There are also three religions in the region, each representing a lake which in turn represents a goddess: Kodave, Yukel, and Enuuk.

The Tyrant Queen
Long ago, before the Day of Ashen Skies, the lands of the lakes were ruled not by its people, but by tyrants. Kings lived in opulence as the people begged for scraps, lords danced in ballrooms as villages burned to ash, and the robber barons squeezed every last clink of coin. But in the year 274 BA came the beginning to the end. A woman, queen and mage, rose to power. Her path to the throne was marred by blood, and greed. Her wars against the other cities left a wake of destruction and misery across the lands, with spoils looted and captives dragged back to her spires in black.

When her ambitions came to light, a desire to achievement Immortality at any cost, the leaders and nobility did not rise to stop her. Instead, the lords sold out their own people, and the mages happily complied for just a chance at her tutelage. It was the common man, ignited by injustice, who united against her. As her armies lost their ground, she unleashed a horde of twisted monstrosities. Their screams were that of the loved, and the lost. The City of Tyranny, from where she ruled, was put to the torch.

Countless were brought to justice as the pyres were lit, and the fires burned bright. Bloodlines ended, and magic all but ceased in these lands. Though many claimed innocence, the trust between the magical and mundane had been shattered. But while dynasties could end, magic seemed like an inevitable force, one which would perpetually remerge. As the bloodlust subsided, the cracks in our methods began to show, a question pierced through the conscience of all the peoples of the lakes: were all mages truly guilty? Could a newborn babe truly bear the burden, the guilt, of its forefathers?

This question has remained with the people of the Triunic Lakes ever since.

Recent History
The Lake Federation is in its infancy, only being formed in 1441. While there were previous precedents of unity such as the Amberpact, none were as ambitious or as encompassing as the federation, which attempted to finally end the petty internal squabbles of the lakes. However, this was only due to the recent Centaur invasion, which happened to be the largest and most devastating in Triunic history.

Nicknamed the Great Incursion, the Centaur Shamans made use of the harshest winter ever recorded by locking the frozen lakes in place and using them as ice bridges, allowing huge centaur hordes to invade, wreaking havoc across the south. The path of blood finally ended in 1440 with the hunting of the last shamans in 1439.

You lead a city-state in the north, the primary supporters of the Lake Federation. With the scars of the previous decade still largely visible in the lands of your southern compatriots and missing men from your fields, the foundation of the federation is shaky at best. Be aware of any crises that may come, as many city-states such as Vahjevgiiv seek to expand their own domain for their own greed. Keep tabs on the Metsamic city-states, or the federation may well collapse. And still, its only a matter of time before the centaurs reorganise and reap a new path of blood once more...

The federation awaits your rule. Will you manage to unify the Triunic Lakes? Or will the city-states burn once more, either by their own hands or under the hooves of the centaurs?

J54.title: The Forbidden Plains, the Centaur Clans: Altorzamh
J54.lore:
"Tens of thousands of Centaurs swarmed across the city-states of the Triunic Lakes, watering the trampled crops with the blood of their former caretakers. None were spared or given the slightest hint of mercy. And thus, an entire generation was lost."
-A Historic Account of the Great Incursion, by Raanu Modsuu (1511 AA)

Primer
The Centaurs of the Forbidden Plains are ancient creatures, originally being Fey, having existed on Halann before the start of recorded history. In fact, the Centaurs today no longer live in their homeland, the Deepwoods in Escann, which used to stretch all the way to the Greatwoods in Gawed.

It was Castan Beastbane who mercilessly slaughtered most of the non-human races in Cannor from 596 to 569 B.A.. Like the Satyrs and Gnolls, the Centaurs almost went extinct in the Second Great Cleansing. However, survivors managed to escape to the Forbidden Plains, where they remain today. Their homeland is no longer recognisable, the majority of the woods having been burnt down.

On the plains, Centaurs have tried to establish a new homeland for themselves, pushing the humans who lived on the mainland to the lakes, where their descendants have formed the Triunic peoples.

Recent History
The Centaurs are typically a quarrelsome race, only having been united twice by Great Caehns during their history on the plains. Both times, the clans have refractured due to their defeat at the hands of the Triunic city-states.

The most recent and most devastating invasion was the Great Incursion, which began in 1433 A.A. and ended in 1440 A.A. The Centaurs took advantage of ancient Fey magic, with the use of Centaur shamans and the most frigid and harsh winter in Triunic history. The shamans locked ice bridges in place, allowing Centaurs to swarm over the sea and attack the humans. However, the clever humans found out about the shamans, and used any means necessary to slay them. In 1439 A.A., the last shaman was killed and the invasion slowly dissipated as the ice bridges melted, causing the remaining warriors to be slaughtered.

Now, you lead one of many Centaur clans, dreaming to unify your people once more. However, the humans have signed a treaty of unity, joining together in the ambitious Lake Federation. O Great Caehn, you must crush these foolish dreams below the thunderous tremor of hundreds of thousands of hooves. Will you unite the clans and conquer once more? Or will your wars cause a Third Great Cleansing? Or perhaps you will succeed... only to fall apart as a war lasts a little too long?

May your enemies tremble before you and lay dying in regret for their savage actions in annihilating your kind. The storm shall never end!

J55.title: The Forbidden Plains, the Centaur Clans: Lejdazamh
J55.lore:
"Tens of thousands of Centaurs swarmed across the city-states of the Triunic Lakes, watering the trampled crops with the blood of their former caretakers. None were spared or given the slightest hint of mercy. And thus, an entire generation was lost."
-A Historic Account of the Great Incursion, by Raanu Modsuu (1511 AA)

Primer
The Centaurs of the Forbidden Plains are ancient creatures, originally being Fey, having existed on Halann before the start of recorded history. In fact, the Centaurs today no longer live in their homeland, the Deepwoods in Escann, which used to stretch all the way to the Greatwoods in Gawed.

It was Castan Beastbane who mercilessly slaughtered most of the non-human races in Cannor from 596 to 569 B.A.. Like the Satyrs and Gnolls, the Centaurs almost went extinct in the Second Great Cleansing. However, survivors managed to escape to the Forbidden Plains, where they remain today. Their homeland is no longer recognisable, the majority of the woods having been burnt down.

On the plains, Centaurs have tried to establish a new homeland for themselves, pushing the humans who lived on the mainland to the lakes, where their descendants have formed the Triunic peoples.

Recent History
The Centaurs are typically a quarrelsome race, only having been united twice by Great Caehns during their history on the plains. Both times, the clans have refractured due to their defeat at the hands of the Triunic city-states.

The most recent and most devastating invasion was the Great Incursion, which began in 1433 A.A. and ended in 1440 A.A. The Centaurs took advantage of ancient Fey magic, with the use of Centaur shamans and the most frigid and harsh winter in Triunic history. The shamans locked ice bridges in place, allowing Centaurs to swarm over the sea and attack the humans. However, the clever humans found out about the shamans, and used any means necessary to slay them. In 1439 A.A., the last shaman was killed and the invasion slowly dissipated as the ice bridges melted, causing the remaining warriors to be slaughtered.

Now, you lead one of many Centaur clans, dreaming to unify your people once more. However, the humans have signed a treaty of unity, joining together in the ambitious Lake Federation. O Great Caehn, you must crush these foolish dreams below the thunderous tremor of hundreds of thousands of hooves. Will you unite the clans and conquer once more? Or will your wars cause a Third Great Cleansing? Or perhaps you will succeed... only to fall apart as a war lasts a little too long?

May your enemies tremble before you and lay dying in regret for their savage actions in annihilating your kind. The storm shall never end!

L25.title: The Forbidden Plains, Ogre Kingdoms: Sakul
L25.lore:
Primer
The Ogres, along with Trolls, are the last of the Giantkin in Halann. The True Giants were one of the original races to inhabit the world, eons ago, but fell in a mutually destructive conflict with the Dragons well before the Day of Ashen Skies. The Ogres claim that they devoured the True Giants and as such gained their strength and power; these cannibalistic beliefs continue to influence their religion (the Feast of the Gods) and culture.

Ogres have long roamed the Forbidden Plains, though the Great Caehn Neagol's conquest of the central steppes coupled with climactic changes after the Day of Ashen Skies led to the Sandmaw Ogres being driven extinct and the Fathide Ogres pushed to the western hills. In AA 28, with the Fathides seemed poised to meet the Sandmaws' fate, their destiny was forever reshaped by the crash-landing of a Sky Giant Fortress into their hills. The inhabitants had perished, and the Ogres gladly feasted on their corpses, regaining their strength and allowing them to survive the long famine years. The chief of the Gravelhide clan, Ghornma Gravelhide, subjugated all the other clans that flocked to the ruins, and formed the kingdom of Maghargma.

Recent History
For centuries, the dominant Ogre Kingdom has remained Maghargma in the south, which makes its capital in the old Sky Giant fortress. In its prime the Kingdom extorted tribute from dozens of other Great Clans, along with a plethora of Centaur tribes who brought slaves and food in return for boons of iron and steel. The northern tribes, however, have remained rebellious ever since AA 125, when Roghrin Gravelhide's rivals fled there following his victory in the Maghargma civil war. The states closer to Maghargma's power base remain loyal, though some of the outlying Great Clans merely pay lip service to their sovereign.

In 1440, however, the same Centaur invasion that forced the Triunic Peoples in the East to unify into the Lake Federation in defense also struck westward, and slew Maghargma's ruler Borigg Gravelhide in battle. His son Ziltagh Gravelhide seized the throne, but he was not another in the long line of Maghargma's rulers who were content to merely sit on their throne and eat. A massive ogre, who crushed centaurs whole with a single blow during his counteroffensive against the Hordes' incursion, his rise has been a check to any rebellious tendencies amongst other Ogres.

Still, every Ogre knows one truth above all: the world is eat or be eaten. Will Ziltagh be able to re-establish Maghargma's dominance, quite literally devour the Forbidden Plains, and even crown himself Mengurkha, ruler of all Ogres? Or will one of their vassals consume the fading state and take up its mantle? Or shall it be one of the Wild Clans in the North, who look south with a blend of fear and ambition and wait for the chance to take a bite—or several—of their own, that shall claim dominance and form Surkokli? The future of the Ogres, and of the entire Forbidden Plains, is up to you.

L68.title: Lizardfolk: Karassk
L68.lore:
“A thousand thousand years the world has lived for, but we have reached the end, for the world awaited me”
- Sykar Riverclaw

Lizardfolk are among the oldest races on Halann. They made their home in Taneyas long before humans, trolls, halflings, and even the genies. Hundreds of lizardfolk empires have risen and fallen in that time, the jungles littered with the fallen cities of old. According to the Anzalkatsa prophecy, all of these empires were destined to fall. Only the final, the eternal, the 333rd Empire will be immune from this collapse. Only the 333rd Empire can unite all lizardfolk and stand tall for the rest of time.

This is the prophecy told by the naga, a unique lizardfolk mutation with large, snake-like bodies and no limbs, but with incredible magical powers. They are shepherds of the prophecy, and will guide the lizardfolk to the eternity of Khatalashya, the 333rd Empire.

The Anzara Empire was the last great empire to be officially counted by the naga. Their collapse came after the Shadowfang Invasion in the year 260. Crocodilian lizardfolk, cursed by the night hags of Yezel Mora with a red mist that clouded their judgement and fueled their rage, rampaged from the Shadow Swamp and across the empire. Anzara split in two, with the emperor moving the capital to Zerat while their eastern holdings stayed under the Kingdom of Shalazar.

Yet Zerat remained tall, refusing to fall into ruin for the next thousand years. In that time only the nation of Karassk rose to try claim the next empire for their own, but their lack of support amongst the naga meant their claim was never recognised. They might have had better luck convincing the rest of Taneyas had their emperor not accidentally blown up the ancient Zatsarya in Ashuzal, the great spires left by the very first lizardfolk empire.

Recent History

When Kheterata dismantled the Karassian Empire and drove them out of Horashesh, it confirmed what many in Taneyas already knew: lizardfolk society is stagnating. Humans, the primitives they once looked down on, have started to claim the jungles off the lizardfolk. Recognising this, the naga have rallied around a new candidate to bring about the next great lizardfolk empire. Not someone from one of the great cities, but instead a lizard from deep in the western jungles: Sykar Riverclaw of Rayaz.

But is the prophecy all nonsense? The crocodilian lizardfolk have found a way to cure their blood curse. Through the pursuit of art, they have begun to learn to quell their rage and have started building a new society, free of any expectations of the prophecy. The rise of Zulbur shows there may be another path for the lizardfolk. Whispers of other ideas have spread too. The shadows whisper, promising great power to those who abandon the prophecy, who abandon the entire idea of a final empire.

What number empire are we on anyway? Well, the Anzara Empire, the legacy of which the nobility in Zerat still cling to, was the 332nd Empire. This means the 333rd Empire, the one kept out of the grasp of Karassk, the one prophesied to unite all lizardfolk and last for eternity, will be the next one. Zerat is on the edge of collapse, with Sykar’s forces recently capturing their ancient capital. The only question left is who will claim the mantle of the Final Empire.

Lizardfolk are among the oldest races on Halann. They made their home in Taneyas long before humans, trolls, halflings, and even the genies. Hundreds of lizardfolk empires have risen and fallen in that time, the jungles littered with the fallen cities of old. According to the Anzalkatsa prophecy, all of these empires were destined to fall. Only the final, the eternal, the 333rd Empire will be immune from this collapse. Only the 333rd Empire can unite all lizardfolk and stand tall for the rest of time.

This is the prophecy told by the naga, a unique lizardfolk mutation with large, snake-like bodies and no limbs, but with incredible magical powers. They are shepherds of the prophecy, and will guide the lizardfolk to the eternity of Khatalashya, the 333rd Empire.

The Anzara Empire was the last great empire to be officially counted by the naga. Their collapse came after the Shadowfang Invasion in the year 260. Crocodilian lizardfolk, cursed by the night hags of Yezel Mora with a red mist that clouded their judgement and fueled their rage, rampaged from the Shadow Swamp and across the empire. Anzara split in two, with the emperor moving the capital to Zerat while their eastern holdings stayed under the Kingdom of Shalazar.

Yet Zerat remained tall, refusing to fall into ruin for the next thousand years. In that time only the nation of Karassk rose to try claim the next empire for their own, but their lack of support amongst the naga meant their claim was never recognised. They might have had better luck convincing the rest of Taneyas had their emperor not accidentally blown up the ancient Zatsarya in Ashuzal, the great spires left by the very first lizardfolk empire.

Recent History

When Kheterata dismantled the Karassian Empire and drove them out of Horashesh, it confirmed what many in Taneyas already knew: lizardfolk society is stagnating. Humans, the primitives they once looked down on, have started to claim the jungles off the lizardfolk. Recognising this, the naga have rallied around a new candidate to bring about the next great lizardfolk empire. Not someone from one of the great cities, but instead a lizard from deep in the western jungles: Sykar Riverclaw of Rayaz.

But is the prophecy all nonsense? The crocodilian lizardfolk have found a way to cure their blood curse. Through the pursuit of art, they have begun to learn to quell their rage and have started building a new society, free of any expectations of the prophecy. The rise of Zulbur shows there may be another path for the lizardfolk. Whispers of other ideas have spread too. The shadows whisper, promising great power to those who abandon the prophecy, who abandon the entire idea of a final empire.

What number empire are we on anyway? Well, the Anzara Empire, the legacy of which the nobility in Zerat still cling to, was the 332nd Empire. This means the 333rd Empire, the one kept out of the grasp of Karassk, the one prophesied to unite all lizardfolk and last for eternity, will be the next one. Zerat is on the edge of collapse, with Sykar’s forces recently capturing their ancient capital. The only question left is who will claim the mantle of the Final Empire.

M05.lore:
"Always keep three daggers hidden on you. One for your enemy, one for your friend, and another to stab yourself and frame your boss."
-Rule 222 in "Da Ultimate Rulez for Survival" by Muk the Bloodless, died of blood loss 1571.

Primer
Born as one of, if not the weakest race, goblins rely on pragmatism, cunning, and extreme cruelty (even when dealing with kin) to gain every advantage, a survival instinct that has permeated deep into goblin society where lives are just a relatively cheap commodity. They are known to live in innumerable numbers inside the Serpentspine's dark depths, and claim to have existed in the mountains long before the dwarven empire Aul-Dwarov dominated the region. Despite that empire's advanced technology, they remained a constant thorn and irritant, raiding unprotected Er-Natvir trains and ambushing any dwarves who strayed too far outside the walls of a Hold. And, of course, they would outlast that decrepit empire, watching with glee during the millennia-long Last Days of the Dwarovar.

When the orcs emerged however, rushing out of Hul-Jorkad, the goblins faced a new threat. They enslaved countless clans, and many were slaughtered even as they achieved the long-held dream of seeing the dwarven holds smashed before them (some, like Verkal Kozenad, fell fast; others, like Gor Ozumbrog, only fell to a goblin horde when betrayed by its allies).

That history meant many goblins knew what was coming when the Dookanson rose and united the orcs behind him. Some fled, retreating to the darkest and most inhospitable caves. Others escaped the Serpentspine, flooding into Bulwar and Bahar as the Exodus Goblins and leading to the Decades of Devastation. The most unfortunate, however, could not escape the Dookanson's slavery and became nothing but cannon fodder and servants during the Greentide.

Recent History
In the end, however, goblins outlasted the Dookanson, just as they outlasted Aul-Dwarov. Now, for the first time in many centuries, goblins are free from the oppression of any empire. Among others, Railskulker has claimed the ancient Railyards, Snotfinger plans revenge on the despised orcs, and Greedy Grin lusts for the gold of Verkal Gulan.

But the fate of the goblin race remains uncertain. Greedy dwarven adventurers from the outside world invade the Serpentspine once again, while decrepit Remnant Holds are bright with light, seemingly rousing from their long sleep. Meanwhile, word from the east says that goblins have been subjugated there by a hobgoblin Command.

No more! The goblins will reclaim their homes once and for all. They will rise from the obscure darkness and unite the tribes into an empire that will rule the whole of Serpentspine. By conquest, plunder, flames, explosions, and whatever other schemes can be dreamed up, it is now up to you to claim the mountains and see goblins rise as they never have before.

M50.title: The Forbidden Plains, the Centaur Clans
M50.lore:
"Tens of thousands of Centaurs swarmed across the city-states of the Triunic Lakes, watering the trampled crops with the blood of their former caretakers. None were spared or given the slightest hint of mercy. And thus, an entire generation was lost."
-A Historic Account of the Great Incursion, by Raanu Modsuu (1511 AA)

Primer
The Centaurs of the Forbidden Plains are ancient creatures, originally being Fey, having existed on Halann before the start of recorded history. In fact, the Centaurs today no longer live in their homeland, the Deepwoods in Escann, which used to stretch all the way to the Greatwoods in Gawed.

It was Castan Beastbane who mercilessly slaughtered most of the non-human races in Cannor from 596 to 569 B.A.. Like the Satyrs and Gnolls, the Centaurs almost went extinct in the Second Great Cleansing. However, survivors managed to escape to the Forbidden Plains, where they remain today. Their homeland is no longer recognisable, the majority of the woods having been burnt down.

On the plains, Centaurs have tried to establish a new homeland for themselves, pushing the humans who lived on the mainland to the lakes, where their descendants have formed the Triunic peoples.

Recent History
The Centaurs are typically a quarrelsome race, only having been united twice by Great Caehns during their history on the plains. Both times, the clans have refractured due to their defeat at the hands of the Triunic city-states.

The most recent and most devastating invasion was the Great Incursion, which began in 1433 A.A. and ended in 1440 A.A. The Centaurs took advantage of ancient Fey magic, with the use of Centaur shamans and the most frigid and harsh winter in Triunic history. The shamans locked ice bridges in place, allowing Centaurs to swarm over the sea and attack the humans. However, the clever humans found out about the shamans, and used any means necessary to slay them. In 1439 A.A., the last shaman was killed and the invasion slowly dissipated as the ice bridges melted, causing the remaining warriors to be slaughtered.

Now, you lead one of many Centaur clans, dreaming to unify your people once more. However, the humans have signed a treaty of unity, joining together in the ambitious Lake Federation. O Great Caehn, you must crush these foolish dreams below the thunderous tremor of hundreds of thousands of hooves. Will you unite the clans and conquer once more? Or will your wars cause a Third Great Cleansing? Or perhaps you will succeed... only to fall apart as a war lasts a little too long?

May your enemies tremble before you and lay dying in regret for their savage actions in annihilating your kind. The storm shall never end!

The Centaurs are typically a quarrelsome race, only having been united twice by Great Caehns during their history on the plains. Both times, the clans have refractured due to their defeat at the hands of the Triunic city-states.

The most recent and most devastating invasion was the Great Incursion, which began in 1433 A.A. and ended in 1440 A.A. The Centaurs took advantage of ancient Fey magic, with the use of Centaur shamans and the most frigid and harsh winter in Triunic history. The shamans locked ice bridges in place, allowing Centaurs to swarm over the sea and attack the humans. However, the clever humans found out about the shamans, and used any means necessary to slay them. In 1439 A.A., the last shaman was killed and the invasion slowly dissipated as the ice bridges melted, causing the remaining warriors to be slaughtered.

Now, you lead one of many Centaur clans, dreaming to unify your people once more. However, the humans have signed a treaty of unity, joining together in the ambitious Lake Federation. O Great Caehn, you must crush these foolish dreams below the thunderous tremor of hundreds of thousands of hooves. Will you unite the clans and conquer once more? Or will your wars cause a Third Great Cleansing? Or perhaps you will succeed... only to fall apart as a war lasts a little too long?

May your enemies tremble before you and lay dying in regret for their savage actions in annihilating your kind. The storm shall never end!

M63.title: Lizardfolk
M63.lore:
“A thousand thousand years the world has lived for, but we have reached the end, for the world awaited me”
- Sykar Riverclaw

Lizardfolk are among the oldest races on Halann. They made their home in Taneyas long before humans, trolls, halflings, and even the genies. Hundreds of lizardfolk empires have risen and fallen in that time, the jungles littered with the fallen cities of old. According to the Anzalkatsa prophecy, all of these empires were destined to fall. Only the final, the eternal, the 333rd Empire will be immune from this collapse. Only the 333rd Empire can unite all lizardfolk and stand tall for the rest of time.

This is the prophecy told by the naga, a unique lizardfolk mutation with large, snake-like bodies and no limbs, but with incredible magical powers. They are shepherds of the prophecy, and will guide the lizardfolk to the eternity of Khatalashya, the 333rd Empire.

The Anzara Empire was the last great empire to be officially counted by the naga. Their collapse came after the Shadowfang Invasion in the year 260. Crocodilian lizardfolk, cursed by the night hags of Yezel Mora with a red mist that clouded their judgement and fueled their rage, rampaged from the Shadow Swamp and across the empire. Anzara split in two, with the emperor moving the capital to Zerat while their eastern holdings stayed under the Kingdom of Shalazar.

Yet Zerat remained tall, refusing to fall into ruin for the next thousand years. In that time only the nation of Karassk rose to try claim the next empire for their own, but their lack of support amongst the naga meant their claim was never recognised. They might have had better luck convincing the rest of Taneyas had their emperor not accidentally blown up the ancient Zatsarya in Ashuzal, the great spires left by the very first lizardfolk empire.

Recent History

When Kheterata dismantled the Karassian Empire and drove them out of Horashesh, it confirmed what many in Taneyas already knew: lizardfolk society is stagnating. Humans, the primitives they once looked down on, have started to claim the jungles off the lizardfolk. Recognising this, the naga have rallied around a new candidate to bring about the next great lizardfolk empire. Not someone from one of the great cities, but instead a lizard from deep in the western jungles: Sykar Riverclaw of Rayaz.

But is the prophecy all nonsense? The crocodilian lizardfolk have found a way to cure their blood curse. Through the pursuit of art, they have begun to learn to quell their rage and have started building a new society, free of any expectations of the prophecy. The rise of Zulbur shows there may be another path for the lizardfolk. Whispers of other ideas have spread too. The shadows whisper, promising great power to those who abandon the prophecy, who abandon the entire idea of a final empire.

What number empire are we on anyway? Well, the Anzara Empire, the legacy of which the nobility in Zerat still cling to, was the 332nd Empire. This means the 333rd Empire, the one kept out of the grasp of Karassk, the one prophesied to unite all lizardfolk and last for eternity, will be the next one. Zerat is on the edge of collapse, with Sykar’s forces recently capturing their ancient capital. The only question left is who will claim the mantle of the Final Empire.

R65.lore:
"Always keep three daggers hidden on you. One for your enemy, one for your friend, and another to stab yourself and frame your boss."
-Rule 222 in "Da Ultimate Rulez for Survival" by Muk the Bloodless, died of blood loss 1571.

Primer
Born as one of, if not the weakest race, goblins rely on pragmatism, cunning, and extreme cruelty (even when dealing with kin) to gain every advantage, a survival instinct that has permeated deep into goblin society where lives are just a relatively cheap commodity. They are known to live in innumerable numbers inside the Serpentspine's dark depths, and claim to have existed in the mountains long before the dwarven empire Aul-Dwarov dominated the region. Despite that empire's advanced technology, they remained a constant thorn and irritant, raiding unprotected Er-Natvir trains and ambushing any dwarves who strayed too far outside the walls of a Hold. And, of course, they would outlast that decrepit empire, watching with glee during the millennia-long Last Days of the Dwarovar.

When the orcs emerged however, rushing out of Hul-Jorkad, the goblins faced a new threat. They enslaved countless clans, and many were slaughtered even as they achieved the long-held dream of seeing the dwarven holds smashed before them (some, like Verkal Kozenad, fell fast; others, like Gor Ozumbrog, only fell to a goblin horde when betrayed by its allies).

That history meant many goblins knew what was coming when the Dookanson rose and united the orcs behind him. Some fled, retreating to the darkest and most inhospitable caves. Others escaped the Serpentspine, flooding into Bulwar and Bahar as the Exodus Goblins and leading to the Decades of Devastation. The most unfortunate, however, could not escape the Dookanson's slavery and became nothing but cannon fodder and servants during the Greentide.

Recent History
In the end, however, goblins outlasted the Dookanson, just as they outlasted Aul-Dwarov. Now, for the first time in many centuries, goblins are free from the oppression of any empire. Among others, Railskulker has claimed the ancient Railyards, Snotfinger plans revenge on the despised orcs, and Greedy Grin lusts for the gold of Verkal Gulan.

But the fate of the goblin race remains uncertain. Greedy dwarven adventurers from the outside world invade the Serpentspine once again, while decrepit Remnant Holds are bright with light, seemingly rousing from their long sleep. Meanwhile, word from the east says that goblins have been subjugated there by a hobgoblin Command.

No more! The goblins will reclaim their homes once and for all. They will rise from the obscure darkness and unite the tribes into an empire that will rule the whole of Serpentspine. By conquest, plunder, flames, explosions, and whatever other schemes can be dreamed up, it is now up to you to claim the mountains and see goblins rise as they never have before.

That history meant many goblins knew what was coming when the Dookanson rose and united the orcs behind him. Some fled, retreating to the darkest and most inhospitable caves. Others escaped the Serpentspine, flooding into Bulwar and Bahar as the Exodus Goblins and leading to the Decades of Devastation. The most unfortunate, however, could not escape the Dookanson's slavery and became nothing but cannon fodder and servants during the Greentide.

Recent History
In the end, however, goblins outlasted the Dookanson, just as they outlasted Aul-Dwarov. Now, for the first time in many centuries, goblins are free from the oppression of any empire. Among others, Railskulker has claimed the ancient Railyards, Snotfinger plans revenge on the despised orcs, and Greedy Grin lusts for the gold of Verkal Gulan.

But the fate of the goblin race remains uncertain. Greedy dwarven adventurers from the outside world invade the Serpentspine once again, while decrepit Remnant Holds are bright with light, seemingly rousing from their long sleep. Meanwhile, word from the east says that goblins have been subjugated there by a hobgoblin Command.

No more! The goblins will reclaim their homes once and for all. They will rise from the obscure darkness and unite the tribes into an empire that will rule the whole of Serpentspine. By conquest, plunder, flames, explosions, and whatever other schemes can be dreamed up, it is now up to you to claim the mountains and see goblins rise as they never have before.

S11.title: Lizardfolk: Sea Roaming Inyubi
S11.lore:
“A thousand thousand years the world has lived for, but we have reached the end, for the world awaited me”
- Sykar Riverclaw

Lizardfolk are among the oldest races on Halann. They made their home in Taneyas long before humans, trolls, halflings, and even the genies. Hundreds of lizardfolk empires have risen and fallen in that time, the jungles littered with the fallen cities of old. According to the Anzalkatsa prophecy, all of these empires were destined to fall. Only the final, the eternal, the 333rd Empire will be immune from this collapse. Only the 333rd Empire can unite all lizardfolk and stand tall for the rest of time.

This is the prophecy told by the naga, a unique lizardfolk mutation with large, snake-like bodies and no limbs, but with incredible magical powers. They are shepherds of the prophecy, and will guide the lizardfolk to the eternity of Khatalashya, the 333rd Empire.

The Anzara Empire was the last great empire to be officially counted by the naga. Their collapse came after the Shadowfang Invasion in the year 260. Crocodilian lizardfolk, cursed by the night hags of Yezel Mora with a red mist that clouded their judgement and fueled their rage, rampaged from the Shadow Swamp and across the empire. Anzara split in two, with the emperor moving the capital to Zerat while their eastern holdings stayed under the Kingdom of Shalazar.

Yet Zerat remained tall, refusing to fall into ruin for the next thousand years. In that time only the nation of Karassk rose to try claim the next empire for their own, but their lack of support amongst the naga meant their claim was never recognised. They might have had better luck convincing the rest of Taneyas had their emperor not accidentally blown up the ancient Zatsarya in Ashuzal, the great spires left by the very first lizardfolk empire.

Recent History

When Kheterata dismantled the Karassian Empire and drove them out of Horashesh, it confirmed what many in Taneyas already knew: lizardfolk society is stagnating. Humans, the primitives they once looked down on, have started to claim the jungles off the lizardfolk. Recognising this, the naga have rallied around a new candidate to bring about the next great lizardfolk empire. Not someone from one of the great cities, but instead a lizard from deep in the western jungles: Sykar Riverclaw of Rayaz.

But is the prophecy all nonsense? The crocodilian lizardfolk have found a way to cure their blood curse. Through the pursuit of art, they have begun to learn to quell their rage and have started building a new society, free of any expectations of the prophecy. The rise of Zulbur shows there may be another path for the lizardfolk. Whispers of other ideas have spread too. The shadows whisper, promising great power to those who abandon the prophecy, who abandon the entire idea of a final empire.

What number empire are we on anyway? Well, the Anzara Empire, the legacy of which the nobility in Zerat still cling to, was the 332nd Empire. This means the 333rd Empire, the one kept out of the grasp of Karassk, the one prophesied to unite all lizardfolk and last for eternity, will be the next one. Zerat is on the edge of collapse, with Sykar’s forces recently capturing their ancient capital. The only question left is who will claim the mantle of the Final Empire.

S22.title: The Adventurer Company: Hisraad
S22.lore:
Primer
Escann was long ago home to the first and greatest empire of humanity: Castanor. It forged a common language across the continent, and drove back both the Deepwoods as well as the fey and centaurs who lived within. However, that greatness faded after the Day of Ashen Skies, and for centuries all that remained were bickering feudal kingdoms, weakened enough to allow Castan Ebonfrost to reunify the empire in his own image when he founded the dreaded Black Castanor.

It only became darker when the 52nd Castan was made a puppet by the Sorcerer-King Nichmer, who then led Black Castanor in brutal wars until most of Cannor fell under his dominion. It would take the continent-spanning War of the Sorcerer-King, and the arrival of Elves, talented in sword and magic, to bring him down.

After Nichmer's death at the Battle of Trialmount, Castanor fractured and Escann entered its chivalric period. Kingdoms like Castellyr, Farraneán, and Adenica became lands of legend — wars were rare and conflicts were instead handled by knightly duels and jousting tournaments.

Recent History
Perhaps this practice made the region weak, as the land crumbled when the Greentide came after the fall of the dwarf hold Khugdihr in 1424. Orcs, united by Korgus Dookanson, rushed out of the Serpentspine in their millions. Castellyr was first to fall, its white walls worthless against this threat from a new direction. The knights of Adenica were slaughtered with their proud steeds, the Blademarches' legendary sword, Calindal, the Gleaming Blade was overcome, and the Marrodic were forced into their mountains.

In the west, as the Lilac Wars raged, many ignored these grim tidings. But following the war's end in 1443, many veterans marched east to confront the threat, earning the moniker "the Marcher Lords". They were joined by a plethora of adventuring companies, from as far afield as Lorent and Kheterata, as well as the Count's League, a remnant band of Castellyrian counts led by Carleon Blacktower, and a force of griffon-riders led by Prince Gryf 'the Young'.

This force dealt the Greentide some of its first defeats, waging a bloody stalemate at the Battle of Rottenstep. Among the many that fell there was Corin, a red-headed squire of Bennon — but that night, she arose as an avatar of the fallen god Agrados. Reborn and renewed, she founded the adventuring party Corin's Circle, rallied the Marcher Lords, and led them all the way to Castonath itself. There, while defending Lothane 'Bluetusk' Silmuna with her shield, and killing Dookanson with her sword, she was slain for the second time. And this time, she would not rise.

That was yesterday, the 10th of Nerament. Now, the morning after, the sun rises upon a new Escann, even if it is still infested by orcish and goblin warbands. Will you reforge one of the kingdoms of old? Or create something new? Perhaps one day all Cannor will again be reshaped by the will of an Escanni Emperor.

S34.title: Lizardfolk: Cunning River Kyil

“A thousand thousand years the world has lived for, but we have reached the end, for the world awaited me”
